entry.title: [SGS] Is this the reason Tom Brady is yet again crashing out online and in real life?
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with their final recap of the World Cup! What a wonderful five weeks it’s been, capped off by a great Spanish victory. The first-ever half-time show was pretty weird, and Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira, BTS, and Coldplay should’ve just played the hits, but at least the Muppets were there. Plus: Trump talked trash about everyone he had to stand next to on stage, including the Spanish Men’s National Team.
Then, the main event: Tom Brady is once again crashing out online and in real life. The gals talk about his recent feud with Logan Paul, which seems to be laying the groundwork for a WWE match. He is also deeply involved with CardVault by Tom Brady, and a series of other strange brand deals that have a lot of people questioning what he’s up to. The gals think they cracked the code.

entry.title: [BOMANI] Why the 1996 USA Team was WAY better than you remember | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Vinnie Goodwill from ESPN for a Time Machine Tuesday edition of The Right Time, diving deep into the history and legacy of the 1996 USA Men's Olympic Basketball Team (often called Dream Team 3). Bo and Vinnie compare the squad to the iconic 1992 Dream Team and the 1994 World Championship team, breaking down the incredible roster that featured young superstars like Penny Hardaway and Grant Hill alongside dominant big men Hakeem Olajuwon, Shaquille O'Neal, and David Robinson.
They discuss why the 1996 team was a physical powerhouse that routinely blew past international competition, how the legendary 1996 free agency period loomed large over the team's tour, and why Charles Barkley managed to be the leading scorer for both the '92 and '96 Olympic runs. Later, they explore the ultimate "what if" regarding Penny Hardaway's injury-shortened prime, the madness surrounding Shaq signing with the Lakers mid-Olympics, and settle the final debate: could the 1996 roster actually beat Michael Jordan and the 1992 Dream Team? . . . Subscribe to Supercast for Ad-Free Episodes: https://righttime.supercast.com/
Buy 'The Right Time' merch: http://therighttimebomani.com/

entry.title: [SERVED] Mary Carillo talks Hall of Fame, Roger Federer, Becoming a Broadcaster & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim talk to Mary Carillo ahead of her well-deserved International Tennis Hall of Fame induction in Newport (alongside one Roger Federer), Mary opens up on 45 years in sports media, 17 Olympics, six Emmys, and the unlikely night at Madison Square Garden that launched it all.
In this episode:
- The Racket Rundown: Rublev, Tsitsipas, Krejčíková, & Merida Titles - The ATP's plan to unify tennis balls by 2029 - Alcaraz's wrist brace at the 2026 World Cup - Mary Carillo on getting into broadcasting - Her legendary storytelling craft and personalizing athletes for the audience - The day lightning struck the broadcast booth while she was inside
Mary's Badminton rant: https://www.nbcnews.com/video/mary-carillo-s-badminton-rant-remains-the-best-sports-commentary-of-all-time-747874883720
COMMENT BELOW: What is your favorite Mary Carillo moment!?

entry.title: [PTFO] Fighting Microtransactions in Video Games with CFB 27 Creator Bordeaux | PTFO
entry.summary:
James Bordeaux was a 23-year-old in Kentucky, playing EA Sports College Football 27 for a living. Then Electronic Arts — the $50-billion mega-developer backed by the Saudis and Jared Kushner's private-equity firm — tried to extract so-called "microtransactions" from inside his beloved video game. So the furious creator put his mouth where his money is: leading the type of organic, populist revolt that has become all too uncommon in American life. Bordeaux explains to Pablo Torre how fans won a battle... but why consumers may never win the war.
• Previously on PTFO — How Microtransactions (Almost) Ruined Gaming, with Dan Soder: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h9vEednqNb0
• Subscribe to Pablo's newsletter for exclusive access, documents and invites: https://www.pablo.show/
• Follow Bordeaux on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@BordeauxYT

entry.title: [FT] Should These MLB Stars Be Traded At The Deadline?
entry.summary:
Kevin Pillar, Todd Frazier and Tricia Whitaker spin the trade deadline wheel to debate whether some of MLB's biggest names should stay put or be moved before the deadline. They weigh in on Kevin Gausman, CJ Abrams, Tarik Skubal, Joe Ryan, José Soriano, Seiya Suzuki and more while making the case for buyers and sellers across the league. Plus, the trio predicts which teams should cash in now and which contenders should hold onto their biggest stars.

entry.title: [UFS] Spain Are World Champions! Messi’s Future, USMNT Reality Check & Pulisic to MLS? | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Spain are World Champions again! On Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, Landon and Tim are joined by former Atlanta United and USMNT goalkeeper Brad Guzan to discuss Spain’s triumph over Argentina, Messi’s last World Cup, and where the USMNT goes from here, including if Mauricio Pochettino stays on as head coach.
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim are LIVE during this summer’s tournament. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. Send your mailbag questions to feedback@unfilteredsoccer.com (https://www.unfilteredsoccer.com).
YouTube Chapters 00:00 Intro 01:52 Spain Win World Cup 09:57 Enzo Fernandez's Red Card 15:13 Now What for Argentina? 25:52 USMNT on USLNT 37:25 Does Pochettino Stay? 43:31 Pulisic to MLS? 1:00:54 Mailbag
#LandonDonovan #TimHoward #USMNT #USSoccer #2026WorldCup #FIFAWorldCup #WorldCup #Messi #BradGuzan #EnzoFernandez #FerranTorres #LamineYamal #MauricioPochettino #ChristianPulisic #SpainWin #SpainWorldChampions
Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard is presented by Volkswagen. Learn more at https://bit.ly/4g8bZG3. Thank you to our additional sponsors: AT&T. Connecting Changes Everything. Visit https://att.com/guarantee to learn more.
Coors Light. Choose Chill. Get Coors Light delivered. Go to CoorsLight.com/USLNT
FanDuel. With FanDuel's Super Sub, if your player is subbed out, your bet stays in. That's right, if your player leaves the match, your bet continues on with the substitute. Download the FanDuel Sportsbook app and place your bets today.
Walmart. Walmart plays on Saturdays. Who knew? Visit walmart.com/MLS
This episode of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard was engineered, edited and mastered by Jeremy Steinkamp with support from Eric Newell. Our producers are Jordan Rizzieri and Andrew Gundling. Our social media lead is Dan Armelli and our social editors are Kyle Curley and Finley Skelton.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal Explains How The Trade Deadline Is Starting To Take Shape
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal discusses why so many teams are still undecided ahead of the MLB Trade Deadline and what that means for buyers and sellers across the league. Ken breaks down the latest on Francisco Lindor and the Mets, why the Reds are open for business, and what makes the Nationals one of the most intriguing teams to watch over the next two weeks. Plus, he explains why the crowded National League Wild Card race could keep the trade market in flux right up until the deadline.

entry.summary:

Plus, Healey shares why the rotation has carried Boston through Garrett Crochet's absence and what the timeline looks like for their ace's return.

entry.title: [FT] Breaking Down Shohei Ohtani's Latest Injury Update & The Dodgers Deadline Outlook
entry.summary:
Todd Frazier, Kevin Pillar and Tricia Whitaker discuss Dave Roberts latest update on Shohei Ohtani after the Dodgers delayed his next start with no timetable for his return to the mound. They explain what the decision means for Ohtani's Cy Young chances and debate who should emerge as the NL Cy Young favorite if he can't get back on track. Plus, they break down whether Los Angeles should be more aggressive at the trade deadline with Will Smith sidelined and questions surrounding the Dodgers rotation.

entry.title: [SERVED] Sinner's 5th Slam, 100th Major Win, and the Case for an All-Time Great

Plus, they break down Sinner's incredible statistics, his place among the all-time greats, and why the future of tennis has never looked brighter.

Plus we’re giving out some awards for the overall tournament and answering YOUR burning questions in the mailbag.

entry.content:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim react to Serena Williams' highly anticipated return to tennis, explain why her comeback is far from over, and what it could mean for the US Open. They also break down Grigor Dimitrov's redemption run, Arthur Fery's breakout Wimbledon performance, and the feel-good stories that made this year's Championships unforgettable.

entry.title: [SERVED] Wimbledon Viewership Ratings, Djokovic Doc, Cincy Entry List & More | 5 Setter
entry.summary:
5 SETTER: This Week in Racket Sports, where we bring you the top five headlines across tennis, padel, pickleball, and more.
Wimbledon 2026 is in the books — Jannik Sinner claimed his fifth Grand Slam title, Serena Williams drew the biggest Wimbledon audience in ESPN history, and the women's final set an all-time record. Plus, Carlos Alcaraz has been cleared to return, and the tennis world cleaned up at the altar.
Check out Andy's Wilson Defyer Review: https://youtu.be/nMXWrYafeAI
In this episode, we cover the biggest stories shaking up the world of racket sports:
1. Wimbledon 2026 viewership records 2. 2026 Cincinnati Open entry list 3. Novak Djokovic "The Wolf in Winter" documentary trailer drops on Prime Video 4. Tennis wedding roundup 5. Anna Leigh Waters' MLP women's doubles win streak snapped at 25-0
If you follow pro tennis, play padel or pickleball, or just want to stay in the loop with the fastest-growing sports on the planet, this is your weekly fix. Subscribe for weekly episodes covering major stories, sharp insights, and fun surprises in the world of racket sports.
COMMENT BELOW What was your favorite racket story from this week?

entry.title: [PTFO] We Investigated (and Interviewed) Epstein's Billionaire Funder | PTFO
entry.summary:
Victoria's Secret mogul turned mega-donor Leslie Wexner introduced Jeffrey Epstein to Harvard. Now, a growing protest movement to strip his name from university buildings is getting shut down. What did Epstein's money man know? When did he know it? And who really calls the shots in higher education? The Harvard Crimson's Dhruv Patel returns for Part 3 in our series on America's most prestigious school and most notorious predator, to examine Wexner and Epstein's "friendship," plus an exclusive interview and a new tranche of internal fundraising transactions that reveals $100 million in publicly unaccounted donations.
• Part 1: WTF Is the Epstein Fund for Women's Sports? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1JclWYNwHQ
• Part 2: New Files Reveal How Epstein Paid Harvard to Launder His Image https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lmOHrXq2jpM
(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and The Harvard Crimson and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

entry.title: [BOMANI] NOTHING has Changed with the Caitlin Clark Experience

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Howard Bryant to break down why the Caitlin Clark discourse keeps getting louder, dumber, and more political. Bo and Howard talk about Clark becoming a culture-war avatar, why the WNBA was always going to push back, whether the noise around her is distorting how people talk about her actual game, and why the Larry Bird comparisons still do not really add up. . . . entry.title: [BOMANI] Messi Stops Time, Is $100M Not Enough? | Bomani Jones & Domonique Foxworth

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Domonique Foxworth for a loaded episode of The Right Time, starting with Lionel Messi and why it feels like the whole game stops when he touches the ball. Bo and Domonique break down Argentina’s late-game magic, why England’s approach fell apart, and how Messi’s gravity bends a match even when he is not dominating every possession. Later, they dive into the athlete-money discourse and explain what players are really trying to say when they claim $100 million does not go as far as fans think.
They talk about why that message always sounds out of touch, how young athletes can burn through huge contracts faster than people realize, why the real issue is lifestyle and financial literacy rather than sympathy, and how the whole conversation connects to athlete podcasts, boss culture, and Jay-Z’s version of capitalism.
. . . Subscribe to Supercast for Ad-Free Episodes: https://righttime.supercast.com/

entry.title: [UFS] Carlos Bocanegra on USMNT's World Cup, Leadership Issues & Fixing Youth Soccer | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
USMNT legend Carlos Bocanegra joined former teammates Landon Donovan and Tim Howard for an in-depth conversation about the United States' 2026 FIFA World Cup run and the future of American soccer.
Carlos shares why he was initially skeptical of the expanded World Cup, why he believes the criticism of the USMNT was unfair, and which players impressed him the most, including Malik Tillman, Weston McKennie, Folarin Balogun, and Alex Freeman.
The trio also discusses leadership within the current squad, Mauricio Pochettino's future, what American players need to do to become stars at Europe's biggest clubs, and how youth soccer in the United States must evolve to compete with the world's elite. Bocanegra also explains why creating the right environment for young talent is critical.
#USMNT #CarlosBocanegra #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #WorldCup #Soccer #Football #Pochettino
Unfiltered Soccer 👉 https://unfilteredsoccer.com/ Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/unfilteredsoccer/ Twitter/X 👉 https://twitter.com/unfilteredsoccr TikTok 👉 https://www.tiktok.com/@unfilteredsoccer Facebook 👉 https://www.facebook.com/UnfilteredSoccer/
OTHER PODCASTS YOU MAY LIKE Triple Option - http://tripleoptionshow.com Throwbacks - http://ThrowbacksShow.com Post Moves - http://PostMovesShow.com Cousins - http://vinceandtmac.com Style of Play - https://www.youtube.com/@styleofplayshow
entry.content:
USMNT legend Carlos Bocanegra joined former teammates Landon Donovan and Tim Howard for an in-depth conversation about the United States' 2026 FIFA World Cup run and the future of American soccer.

Carlos shares why he was initially skeptical of the expanded World Cup, why he believes the criticism of the USMNT was unfair, and which players impressed him the most, including Malik Tillman, Weston McKennie, Folarin Balogun, and Alex Freeman.

The trio also discusses leadership within the current squad, Mauricio Pochettino's future, what American players need to do to become stars at Europe's biggest clubs, and how youth soccer in the United States must evolve to compete with the world's elite. Bocanegra also explains why creating the right environment for young talent is critical.

entry.title: [SGS] Did Bam Adebayo punch Tyler Herro in the face because Herro “doesn’t believe in history?”
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with dear friend of the show, senior NBA reporter/writer Fred Katz! The gals talk to Fred about the biggest stories in the NBA from Summer League and free agency.
First up: Mitchell Robinson was traded from the Knicks to the Celtics, and he is now trolling both Celtics fans and his former teammates. Fred describes Mitch’s persona and tells behind-the-scenes stories about him (including some insights from his forthcoming book about the Knicks!) and everyone spends a surprising amount of time talking about fonts.
Then, the punch heard round the NBA! Bam Adabayo punched his former teammate Tyler Herro in the face at 9am at Summer League in Las Vegas — is it because Tyler Herro “doesn’t believe in history” or anything that happened before 1950? And, finally, what is the angle that LeBron James’s camp is trying to spin with the Sixers talk? Is it real? Or is he just going to Golden State? 0:00 The gals say hello 2:15 Fred Katz joins the show! 6:28 Mitchell Robinson traded 31:16 Bam Adebayo-Tyler Herro fight 46:05 LeBron to Sixers? 1:00:06 Fashion mailbag

entry.title: [PTFO] Decoding the Kawhi Leonard Investigation, with DeMaurice Smith | PTFO
entry.summary:
A superstar held up by the salary cap. A billionaire under the microscope. A league inquiry hidden from public view. De Smith has seen this playbook for punishment before. The former NFL union boss — and white-shoe law-firm investigator — returns to answer questions the NBA still won't: Who is "independent" when the league (and Steve Ballmer, in particular) pays the legal bill? Why would witnesses participate if they face potential criminal exposure? How do unions play the long game? And what obligation does a league have to its fans, if cheating can be the baseline?
• Read "Turf Wars" by DeMaurice Smith: https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/735685/turf-wars-by-demaurice-smith/
• Binge-watch Pablo's Kawhi Leonard investigation: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLp43H6Vt_yDoUarxNb_fVK9_6bSiA2aHo
• Previously on PTFO — Digging Up NFL Collusion Dirt with Ex-Union Boss DeMaurice Smith: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=db4DXqq5iPY
(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

entry.title: [SERVED] Why Do Players Double Fault More On Grass? | Q&Andy
entry.summary:
Want to be featured on the next Q&Andy? Send us a video on our socials or email us at askandy@servedmediagroup.com Andy Roddick answers some of the biggest tennis questions coming out of Wimbledon, from why serve-and-volley has nearly disappeared to whether modern players are simply getting too big, fast, and athletic for the old-school game. He also breaks down Coco Gauff's unique tactical brilliance, explains why players take more risks on grass, shares behind-the-scenes stories about playing on Centre Court, and reflects on the mindset that fueled his career.
📱 Stay up to date with Q&Andy! Follow us on our socials: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast https://x.com/served_podcast

entry.title: [CD] XPeng L03 DRIVEN: Has China SOLVED The Model Y?!
entry.summary:
The XPeng L03 is a $21,000 electric crossover with Model Y-rivalling space, advanced technology, and XPeng’s VLA 2.0 driving system. It costs around $18,000 less than the cheapest Tesla Model Y in China, and it is coming to Europe and other global markets.
XPeng L03 First Look: https://youtu.be/t9Oj4YOhW8A
In this video, I test the XPeng L03 on the road and examine its price, range, charging, interior, practicality, suspension, export specifications, and VLA 2.0 technology. With European pricing yet to be announced, could this half-price Model Y rival expose just how expensive mainstream EVs have become?
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
____________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
- XPeng L03 starts from $18,000 less than Tesla Model Y in China - Uses 2 XPeng Turing AI Chips 1500 TOPs - XPeng VLA 2.0 ADAS (FSD Beta Rival) - Coming to Australia, Europe and 63 countries - XPeng VLA 2.0 coming to Europe in 2027 ____________________________________________________________

entry.title: [SERVED] Sabalenka & Swiatek's Grass Court Struggles, Gauff's Drop Shot & More | Love All w/ Kim Clijsters
entry.summary:
Join 4-time Grand Slam champion Kim Clijsters and tennis reporter Blair Henley as they recap Wimbledon, celebrate champions Jannik Sinner and Linda Nosková, and explore why Czech tennis continues to produce Grand Slam winners. Kim also shares behind-the-scenes stories from the Wimbledon Champions' Ball, returning to the All England Club, and experiencing the tournament from a new perspective after retirement. They also break down the WTA Finals move to Indian Wells, the ATP's proposed doubles overhaul, Roger Federer's upcoming Hall of Fame induction, Tatjana Maria's grass-court success, and Kim's tactical analysis of Aryna Sabalenka and Iga Świątek. Plus, hear why Kim believes Czech coaching remains one of the biggest keys to sustained success in tennis.
Welcome to Love All! If you want to hang out with us behind the scenes follow us on all of our socials: https://www.instagram.com/loveallpodcast/ https://www.tiktok.com/@loveallpodcast https://x.com/loveallpodcast

entry.title: [SGS] Argentina is at the center of World Cup conspiracy theories and an FBI investigation
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back! First, they talk about the lawsuit that uncovered a video Phillies star Bryce Harper made for a FanDuel customer — and Harper’s PR response.
Then, the gals dive into World Cup drama. First, the response to racist remarks by Spain’s former prime minister. Then, they talk about France vs. Spain, and why the US men’s soccer program needs to learn from those countries. Then, a discussion about the controversial calls that led to England and Argentina making it to the semi-finals (involves tin-foil hat theories). The gals also discuss England star Jude Bellingham and his coach, and decide that VAR is the LinkedIn of sports.
Finally, the gals talk about Argentina, because the country’s soccer federation is at the center of an FBI investigation in South Florida.
0:00 The gals say hello 5:04 Bryce Harper drama 16:41 France vs. Spain 23:51 England vs. Argentina 43:32 Argentina’s soccer federation corruption

entry.title: [BOMANI] How the Celtics FAILED Brown & Tatum, Caitlin Clark Discourse Gets More Insane | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Howard Bryant for a loaded episode of The Right Time, starting with why the Boston Celtics failed the Jaylen Brown-Jayson Tatum era. Bo and Howard break down how Boston treated Brown like the expendable star, why the organization clearly picked Tatum as the face of the franchise, and what Tatum now has to prove coming off an Achilles injury. They also get into Brown’s move to Philadelphia, whether the basketball fit makes any sense next to Joel Embiid and Tyrese Maxey, and why the whole breakup felt colder than it needed to be.
Later, Bo and Howard dive into the Caitlin Clark discourse and explain why the conversation around her has somehow gotten even louder, dumber, and more political since she got to the WNBA. They talk about Clark becoming a culture-war avatar, why the league’s player-driven culture was always going to push back, whether her actual game is getting overrated by the noise around her, and why the Larry Bird comparisons don’t really hold up. . Subscribe to Supercast for Ad-Free Episodes: https://righttime.supercast.com/

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 Wimbledon Recap: Serena's Return, Sinner's Historic Win & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim wrap up the 2026 Wimbledon Championships by breaking down the tournament's biggest stories, from Serena Williams' long-awaited return to Jannik Sinner cementing himself as the game's dominant force with another Grand Slam title. They discuss Sinner's historic run, Linda Nosková's emotional championship, Arthur Fery's breakout, Grigor Dimitrov's redemption, Coco Gauff's grass-court breakthrough, the American men's disappointing results, and why the US Open feels more wide open than ever. Plus, they spotlight Tokito Oda, Jordan Lee's junior title, and the next generation shaping tennis' future.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Wimbledon 2026?
📩 Sign up for the Newsletter! https://servedmedia.substack.com/
Check out some Served Merch: https://store.servedpodcast.com/
📱If you're new to the Served with Andy Roddick, we're happy you're here! Follow us for more: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast/ https://x.com/Served_Podcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast
Theme music composed, produced, and mixed by Dan Whittemore. Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwhit.wav/
Learn more about ServiceNow here: https://www.servicenow.com/?campid=27...
TIMESTAMPS: 00:00 – Intro 00:24 – Welcome to Served – Wimbledon two-week recap 00:31 – Serena Williams' comeback story 04:27 – Grigor Dimitrov's redemption run & wild card success stories 06:44 – American men's rough Wimbledon (Tiafoe, Shelton, Fritz) 09:15 – Zverev's run to the final 09:37 – Coco Gauff's tough semifinal loss 11:24 – Parity on the WTA side heading into the US Open 13:02 – Linda Nosková's emotional championship run 16:38 – Sinner's title defense & historic stats 20:50 – Eala Mania and fun Wimbledon storylines 23:06 – Shoutout to wheelchair tennis 24:35 – Boys' junior Wimbledon final recap (Cruz Hewitt, Jordan Lee) 27:34 – Bracket challenge winners announced 29:53 – Closing Thoughts
--- --- ---
Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.

entry.title: [PTFO] How Microtransactions (Almost) Ruined Gaming, with Dan Soder | PTFO
entry.summary:
Virtual currency is a new cheat code. But when EA Sports tried to squeeze extra cash out of college-football fans with so-called "microtransactions" inside the new CFB 27, gamers revolted... and won? Comedian Dan Soder, who's been playing video games with a future NFL coach since childhood, takes Pablo Torre on a journey to the seventh circle of nerddom: What does this phenomenon say about private equity turning fun into an asset? How do we stop sacrificing community for convenience? And is shame still possible?
• Subscribe to Dan Soder on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@DanSoder
Previously on PTFO:
• What It's Like to Play Madden Against an NFL Head Coach: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Drnz-oS6RIU
• We Smoked All the Athlete-Branded Weed We Could Find: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fZcahaB3hYI

entry.title: [FT] Cole Hamels On The 2008 Phillies, Zack Wheeler & This Year's World Series Hopes
entry.summary:
Former MLB All-Star Cole Hamels joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Hamels reflects on the 2008 Phillies, explains why he still roots for the franchise, and shares behind-the-scenes stories from one of the greatest rotations in baseball. Plus, he weighs in on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, discusses how starting pitchers should be developed in today's game, and looks back on some unforgettable moments from his Phillies career.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal On How MLB's Crowded Playoff Race Will Impact The Trade Deadline
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal breaks down how MLB's crowded playoff races could completely reshape this year's trade deadline. With 23 of 30 teams still within four games of a playoff spot entering the All-Star break, Ken explains why buyers and sellers are harder to identify than ever. Plus, he discusses what the Tigers should do with Tarik Skubal, why the Red Sox have changed the conversation, and how teams like the Astros, Blue Jays, Orioles, Diamondbacks and Padres could approach one of the most unpredictable deadlines in recent memory.

entry.title: [BOMANI] Bam Adebayo punches Tyler Herro, Wembanyama's discount, Conor McGregor's next move | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is back from NBA Summer League in Las Vegas with a loaded episode of The Right Time, starting with the wildest story of the weekend: the Bam Adebayo-Tyler Herro fight.
From there, Bo gets into Victor Wembanyama’s five-year, $252 million extension with the San Antonio Spurs and explains why taking less than the full max could be great for the team but dangerous as a precedent for NBA stars. He also talks Summer League standouts, the AJ Dybantsa vs. Darryn Peterson matchup, the ongoing Kawhi Leonard and LA Clippers investigation & whether anyone actually cares how that case ends. He also reacts to Conor McGregor’s latest injury in Las Vegas, and Jaylen Brown’s recent comments.

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON MEN'S FINAL RECAP: Jannik Sinner Wins 2026 Wimbledon, Zverev's Breakthrough & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim break down Jannik Sinner's second straight Wimbledon title, explain why Alexander Zverev may have played one of the smartest matches of his career despite the loss, and put Sinner's fifth Grand Slam into historical perspective. They also discuss Sinner's remarkable resilience after Roland Garros, how his game continues to evolve, and what Carlos Alcaraz's injury means for the rivalry.
COMMENT BELOW: What did you think of the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Final?
📩 Sign up for the Newsletter & Live Chat w/ us during matches! https://servedmedia.substack.com/
📱If you're new to the Served with Andy Roddick, we're happy you're here! Follow us for more: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast/ https://x.com/Served_Podcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast
Learn more about ServiceNow here: https://www.servicenow.com/?campid=27...
--- --- ---
Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.

entry.title: [FT] 10x All-Star Chris Sale Shares Hilarious Stories From His White Sox Days With AJ
entry.summary:
Braves ace Chris Sale joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from MLB All-Star Week in Philadelphia! Sale reflects on earning his 10th All-Star selection, looks back on his first Midsummer Classic alongside legends like Derek Jeter, Justin Verlander and David Ortiz, and discusses what it means to still be pitching at an elite level. Plus, Sale shares his thoughts on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, reveals whether he still has another gear on the mound, and tells hilarious stories from his early days with A.J. in Chicago.

entry.title: [FT] Max Muncy on His All-Star Experience, Ohtani's Knee Injury & The Dodgers Confidence
entry.summary:
Dodgers third baseman Max Muncy joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Muncy shares what it's like preparing for another All-Star Game, explains why he turned down the Home Run Derby, and reveals why he thinks one of the hometown Phillies has the edge to win it. Plus, he provides an update on Shohei Ohtani's knee, discusses the Dodgers confidence despite a rough finish before the break, and reflects on celebrating another World Series title with a White House visit.

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON WOMEN'S FINAL RECAP: Nosková's Wild Comeback, Sinner vs Zverev Preview & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim react to one of the most emotional Wimbledon women's finals in recent memory, breaking down Linda Nosková's remarkable mental recovery after letting five championship points slip away before capturing her first Grand Slam title. They also explore the Czech Republic's remarkable tennis pipeline, debate whether runner-up speeches should remain mandatory, and preview the men's final with a deep tactical breakdown of Jannik Sinner vs. Alexander Zverev, including why grass could finally change their rivalry.
COMMENT BELOW: What did you think of the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Final?

Learn more about ServiceNow here: https://www.servicenow.com/?campid=27...
--- --- ---
Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 12 RECAP: Djokovic's Three-Year Slam Drought, Sinner's Drop Shot & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim break down Day 12 at Wimbledon as Jannik Sinner delivers another masterclass against Novak Djokovic, Alexander Zverev cruises into the final, and the conversation shifts to what Djokovic's future—and Sinner's dominance—really mean for men's tennis. They also preview the all-Czech women's semifinal between Karolína Muchová and Linda Nosková, explain why Czech players continue to thrive on grass, analyze Sinner's tactical evolution, debate Novak's place in the modern game, and reveal what fans should watch for as Wimbledon reaches its final weekend.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 12?
📩 Sign up for the Newsletter & Live Chat w/ us during matches! https://servedmedia.substack.com/

entry.title: [SERVED] Is This Wilson Racket a CHEAT CODE for Any Player?
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick thought he'd never hit a backhand like this again -- until Wilson's Ron Rocchi walked in with a prototype he wasn't even allowed to keep.
This is a rare, up-close look at a Hall of Fame player hitting with a brand-new racket for the very first time... and absolutely loving it. Watch Andy Roddick and Ron Rocchi, Global Tour Manager at Wilson, break down the technology behind @WilsonRacquet's newest frame -- why the game has changed with fluffing balls, slower courts, and bigger, stronger players, and why half of Wilson's tour athletes asked for an equipment change last season.
Then Andy takes the new racket -- built around a bigger, more uniform sweet spot and next-level torsional stability -- out on court, and the reaction speaks for itself. Plus the pros already switching to it, and the one frame Ron refused to let Andy walk away with.
COMMENT BELOW: What racket are you playing with right now -- and are you due for an upgrade?
Check out Wilson's incredible line of rackets here: https://www.wilson.com/en-us/tennis

entry.title: [PTFO] Reviewing Sports Trophies with Unfiltered Art Critic Jerry Saltz | PTFO

entry.title: [SERVED] Can Karolína Muchová Win Wimbledon? Coco Gauff's Heartbreak & More | 2026 Wimbledon Day 11 Recap
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim break down Day 11 of Wimbledon, analyzing Coco Gauff's painful semifinal loss to Karolína Muchová, previewing the blockbuster Novak Djokovic vs. Jannik Sinner showdown, and debating whether Arthur Fery's dream run can continue against Alexander Zverev.
They reveal why Gauff's infamous drop shot wasn't necessarily the wrong decision, explain the tactical chess match that could decide the men's semifinals, dive into Muchová's all-court brilliance, discuss Wimbledon court conditions, and share behind-the-scenes stories from life inside the Championships.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 11?

entry.title: [BOMANI] Vernon Maxwell GOES OFF on Celtics trading Jaylen Brown, Spurs Collapse vs. Knicks | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Vernon “Mad Max” Maxwell for a long, funny, and extremely candid NBA conversation on this episode of The Right Time. Maxwell returns to the show to talk about his second act in sports media, how he still trains like a player, and why he believes some of the biggest storylines in the league are being misunderstood.
Bomani and Max dive deep into the Jalen Brown conversation, including why Maxwell remains so high on Brown as a player, why he thinks the criticism has gone too far, and what Brown could look like in Philadelphia alongside Tyrese Maxey and Joel Embiid. They also get into what Boston may have gotten wrong, whether Jayson Tatum should have done more to keep Brown around, and why the Celtics’ decision still doesn’t sit right with Max.
The conversation also covers Hakeem Olajuwon’s one-of-one greatness, Giannis Antetokounmpo trade speculation involving the Miami Heat, LeBron James’ future after the Lakers, the appeal of a Jokic-LeBron fit in Denver, LaMelo Ball’s evolution, Jalen Brunson’s footwork and mentality, and why Victor Wembanyama could be on the verge of becoming the face of the NBA.
0:00 Intro 4:53 Why Vernon Maxwell puts Jalen Brown in his top five 9:32 Why moving on from Brown never made sense to 16:18 The real Sixers question 21:03 Giannis-to-Miami talk and what the Heat roster would still need 32:48 Where LeBron should go next 37:30 Spurs-Knicks Finals talk 42:21 Is this about to become Victor Wembanyama’s league?

entry.title: [SGS] Norway’s Erling Haaland had crazy lore before he became a World Cup star
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a jam-packed episode. First, some updates about the World Cup. Then, a deep dive into the lore of Norway’s star player, Erling Haaland, from his Snapchat and YouTube vlogs, to his shopping trips in Dallas for cowboy hats, to his Birkin bags, to his cool soccer-playing girlfriend Isabel Haugseng Johansen.
Then, an update on some compressed flowers to debate: Lions wide receiver Amon-Ra St. Brown and Brooklyn Adams’ engagement, and Falcons wide receiver Drake London and Sofia Rose Gabay’s engagement.

Why were Taylor Swift's wedding guests and celebrity Knicks fans assigned "risk" scores inside Madison Square Garden's secret VIP database? Who was the karaoke MVP at the Taylor Swift / Travis Kelce wedding afterparty? And would you rather test-drive the "most potent weed beverage known to man" ... or Bill Simmons' colonoscopy? Plus: Guy Fieri, Shaboozey, Polyp George, The Mike Vrabel Sliding Scale of Chivalry, The Blandino Tier and fogging the glass of exclusivity.

entry.content:
Why were Taylor Swift's wedding guests and celebrity Knicks fans assigned "risk" scores inside Madison Square Garden's secret VIP database? Who was the karaoke MVP at the Taylor Swift / Travis Kelce wedding afterparty? And would you rather test-drive the "most potent weed beverage known to man" ... or Bill Simmons' colonoscopy? Plus: Guy Fieri, Shaboozey, Polyp George, The Mike Vrabel Sliding Scale of Chivalry, The Blandino Tier and fogging the glass of exclusivity.

• Madison Square Garden Kept a List of Gay Celebrities (Wired): https://www.wired.com/story/madison-square-garden-celebrity-database-surveillance

• Previously on PTFO — Knicks Surveillance, Revealed: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yNZ3hJPN5Vc

• Watch Jay Jurden's stand-up special: https://www.hulu.com/movie/jay-jurden-yes-maam-ba411b28-b68b-4884-91f0-ee8a5c7bed2b

entry.title: [CD] This NIO ES8 Has Fewer Seats And More EVERYTHING!
entry.summary:
The NIO ES8 5-seater takes NIO’s luxury electric SUV and makes it more spacious, more practical, and arguably more interesting. By removing the third row, NIO has created huge rear-seat space, a massive boot, and a five-seat version that feels like more than just a seat option.
NIO ES8 full Review: https://youtu.be/RNIcmEhxSgY
In this video, I look at what makes the NIO ES8 five-seat version different from the six-seat and seven-seat models, including the rear-seat layout, luxury features, cargo space, fridge, massage seats, and the latest NIO World Model ADAS update.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
____________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
* NIO ES8 five-seat luxury electric SUV * Same 5.28m body as the six-seat and seven-seat ES8 * Huge second-row legroom with reclining rear bench * HUGE Trunk space * Latest NIO World Model ADAS update
____________________________________________________________

entry.title: [FT] Anthony Volpe Shuts Down False Report From Michael Kay
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Alanna Rizzo react to Anthony Volpe shutting down a report that claimed he refused to play second base in the minors after Michael Kay publicly retracted the story. The trio discusses why a player's reputation matters, the responsibility that comes with anonymous sourcing, and why Volpe's response says a lot about the kind of teammate he is. Plus, they debate Don Mattingly's future with the Phillies and whether a deep postseason run should earn him the full-time manager's job.

entry.title: [FT] Former MLB GM Jim Duquette Predicts MLB's Biggest Trade Deadline Moves
entry.summary:
Former Mets GM Jim Duquette joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Alanna Rizzo to reveal which stars could be on the move before the MLB trade deadline. Jim predicts Bo Bichette could be the biggest name traded, explains why the Orioles have shifted into sell mode, discusses the latest on Taylor Ward, Jarren Duran and Kevin Gausman, and breaks down why the Athletics could surprise everyone. Plus, he shares why the Mets' long-term future with Francisco Lindor remains worth watching.

entry.title: [FT] Why Dylan Cease Has Been Worth Every Penny For Toronto
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Alanna Rizzo react to Dylan Cease falling just three outs shy of a no-hitter and debate whether the Blue Jays made the right decision letting him chase history. Erik, Kevin and Alanna discuss why Cease's durability, dominant stuff and the timing of the season made the decision an easy one, where he stands in the American League Cy Young race, and why Toronto's early investment in the right-hander is paying off. Plus, they examine how postseason workloads can influence managers' decisions when pitchers have a chance to make history.

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 10 RECAP: Fery's Historic Semifinal Run, Kostyuk's Redemption Arc & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim break down Day 10 at Wimbledon, from Arthur Fery's unbelievable home-soil fairytale and Alexander Zverev's semifinal run to Coco Gauff's growing confidence on grass and the fascinating women's semifinal matchups. They analyze why variety is thriving at Wimbledon, debate who has the edge heading into Championship Weekend, explain what makes Fery's run historically unique, and share behind-the-scenes stories from the All England Club.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 10?

Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.

entry.title: [BOMANI] LeBron to Cleveland Again? Why Bomani Jones Isn’t Sold

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones & Danny Parkins break down the biggest LeBron landing spots and why a return to Cleveland, a reunion with Miami, or even a surprise move elsewhere all come with very different stakes.
This clip dives into whether LeBron still changes everything on a minimum deal, why the Harden fit in Cleveland feels shaky, and how teams like Miami, Golden State, San Antonio, Denver, and Minnesota all create fascinating what-if scenarios.

entry.title: [SERVED] Marta Kostyuk on Ukraine's Resilience & Her Breakthrough Season
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim sat down with Marta Kostyuk to discuss the breakthrough season that's taken her to the top of the game. Kostyuk opened up about the mindset shift behind her consistency, why working with coach Sandra Zaniewska changed everything, the difficult decision to skip Rome before Roland Garros, and what it means to represent Ukraine on the biggest stages in tennis while her family remains back home.
This episode was released on May 19, 2026.
To watch the full episode, click here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=01rNgQjNRJ0

entry.title: [UFS] Pulisic Under Fire, Messi’s Relief-Tears & Bellingham’s Masterclass | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
It’s time for the World Cup quarterfinals! On Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, Landon and Tim discuss if England and Jude Bellingham can get past Erling Haaland and Norway, what's going on with Alphonso Davies and Jesse Marsch in Canada, and if Messi and Argentina are lucky to have gotten past Egypt.
Plus, in our mailbag the guys dive into what went wrong with Christian Pulisic, whether or not Diego Luna would have made a difference in the Belgium game, and why Ricardo Pepi and Haji Wright weren’t good subs for Flo Balogun.
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim are LIVE during this summer’s tournament. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. Send your mailbag questions to feedback@unfilteredsoccer.com (https://www.unfilteredsoccer.com).

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 Wimbledon Day 9 Recap: Djokovic's Marathon Win, Coco Gauff's Redemption & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick reacts to Day 9 of Wimbledon, breaking down Novak Djokovic's record-setting five-hour epic against Félix Auger-Aliassime, Coco Gauff's statement victory over Jessica Pegula, and why Karolína Muchová may be the most complete player left in the women's draw. Andy also shares what it was like calling Djokovic's historic match for ESPN, previews Taylor Fritz vs. Alexander Zverev, explains why Jannik Sinner's dominance is becoming almost impossible to appreciate in real time, and discusses which players now have the clearest path to lifting the Wimbledon trophy.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 9?

entry.title: [SGS] Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding recap: Tom Brady’s paparazzi photo and Mike Vrabel’s PR tour
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a Tayvis Wedding Extravaganza! But first: The United States dabbled in FIFA corruption, which means America is officially a soccer country. Donald Trump called FIFA president, Gianni Infantino, and got Flo Balogun’s red card un-suspended (the gals recorded this before the game last night, but they did have a feeling it wouldn’t go well). They also talk about Norway’s Erling Haaland, the great Messi, and who will win it all.
Then it’s Tayvis time! The gals talk about the sportiest guests at the wedding, from Mike Vrabel, to Tom Brady, to Dean Blandino — and if you don’t know who Dean Blandino is, keep listening. The gals also discuss the business of Taylor Swift, the logistics of a wedding like this, Lena Dunham’s football speech, and much, much more!
0:00 The gals say hello 4:00 USA red card drama 19:59 More World Cup updates 28:24 Taylor-Travis wedding!

entry.title: [BOMANI] USMNT DISASTER vs. Belgium, Donovan Mitchell's absurd contract, LeBron's next move | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Danny Parkins of First Things First. They start with Team USA’s loss to Belgium and what it says about the current state of American soccer. They break down the Balogun red-card drama, whether the U.S. benefited from a favorable draw, why this might still be the most talented team the country has ever put on the field, and whether the American youth soccer system is actually built to develop top-level players.
From there, they shift to the NBA and dig into the Lakers’ roster, Luka’s fit, Bronny’s possible role, and what kind of team Los Angeles is really trying to build. They also talk through the Celtics’ roster decisions, the changing math of paying star players, and the most fascinating possible next chapter for LeBron.

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 Wimbledon Day 8 Recap | Arthur Fery's Dream Run, De Minaur's Emotional Exit & More |
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim recap Day 8 at the 2026 Wimbledon Championships, breaking down Flavio Cobolli's breakout win, Taylor Fritz's dominant form, Arthur Fery's dream run, and Alex de Minaur's candid post-match comments. They also discuss Jasmine Paolini's resurgence, the evolving women's draw, Grigor Dimitrov's comeback, and who looks best positioned to win Wimbledon.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 8?

entry.title: [BOMANI] Why Jaylen Brown ACTUALLY got traded, LeBron James next move, Balogun returns for US | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones breaks down the Jaylen Brown trade, why the Boston Celtics decided to move on, and what the deal says about the NBA’s new era of team-building, player value, and trade season. On this episode of The Right Time, Bomani explains why Jaylen Brown remains a highly productive scorer while also unpacking the bigger debate around advanced stats, roster construction, and the never-ending “athletes vs. math elites” conversation.
Bomani also looks at what happens next for LeBron James, why his next move may be about more than basketball, and which teams make the most sense as the NBA keeps orbiting around his future.
Plus, Bomani gets into geopolitics at the World Cup, and a hilarious reaction to Trump supporters getting stuck inside the Smithsonian National Museum of African American History and Culture during a July 4 storm.
Subscribe to Supercast for Ad-Free Episodes: https://righttime.supercast.com/

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 7 RECAP: Osaka Stuns Sabalenka, Novak Passes Federer’s Record & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim break down one of the biggest days of Wimbledon 2026, from Naomi Osaka's stunning upset of Aryna Sabalenka and Novak Djokovic's record-setting run to Félix Auger-Aliassime's controversial battle with Alejandro Davidovich Fokina. They also debate Jessica Pegula's championship chances, analyze Coco Gauff's best performance of the tournament, and discuss the increasingly unpredictable women's draw.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 7?
📩 Sign up for the Newsletter & Live Chat w/ us during matches! https://servedmedia.substack.com/
📱If you're new to the Served with Andy Roddick, we're happy you're here! Follow us for more: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast/ https://x.com/Served_Podcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast
Learn more about ServiceNow here: https://www.servicenow.com/?campid=27...
--- --- ---
Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.
--- --- ---

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 6 RECAP: Rybakina OUT, Dimitrov's Comeback & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim react to the storylines of Day 6 at Wimbledon, breaking down Iga Świątek's upset loss, Grigor Dimitrov's comeback, Frances Tiafoe's early exit, and the women's draw opening up. They also discuss Madison Keys' title chances, Alexandra Eala's breakout run, Taylor Fritz's path forward, and Serena Williams' doubles withdrawal with Venus.
COMMENT BELOW: Who do you have winning your re-draw bracket?

entry.title: [SERVED] Alex Eala talks Filipino Pride & Handling The Pressure of Fame
entry.summary:
Alex Eala joins Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim to talk about her tennis journey so far. Eala shares why Filipinos have a one-of-a-kind sense of community, compares the energy to Manny Pacquiao fight nights, and gets honest about balancing fame with the daily grind of competing at the highest level. Alex reveals which past legends she'd love to have faced (Serena? Sharapova?), the one player she's desperate to match up against on tour right now, and the specific parts of her game she's locked in on improving.
This episode was released on March 24, 2026.
Check out the full interview here: https://youtu.be/U1rcV6AlsQo

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 5 RECAP: Djokovic Ties Federer's Wimbledon Wins Record, Gauff's Grind & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and JW break down Wimbledon Day 5, from Novak Djokovic tying Roger Federer's all-time Wimbledon win record to Naomi Osaka's dominant surge and Jenson Brooksby's inspiring run through the draw. They dig into Jan-Lennard Struff's upset of Daniil Medvedev, preview a red-hot Sabalenka-Osaka showdown, and unpack Coco Gauff's grinding win.

entry.title: [RTRS] Reconciling with the Jaylen Brown Trade

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
After sleeping on it, Mike has had a change of heart. He comes to you all today to take the steps towards healing, reconciliation, and a more positive path forward. He is joined by Brandon Gowton and Roy Burton to rehash the Jaylen Brown trade, JB’s post trade livestream, where Embiid fits in, signing Anfernee Simons, the importance of colonoscopies, and the potential arrival of LeBron James.
Thanks to Brandon Lee Gowton from Bleeding Green Nation and Roy Burton, freelance contributor for PHLY, for joining the pod today.
The Rights To Ricky Sanchez is presented by Draft Kings Surfside Iced Tea and Vodka is the official canned cocktail of The Ricky LL Pavorsky Jewelers is where Rights To Ricky Sanchez listeners go and get engaged. Kornblau Law is the official law firm of the process

entry.title: [PTFO] Uncovering Phil Mickelson's Misconduct Allegations | PTFO
entry.summary:
After months of rumors, journalist Alan Shipnuck investigates why Phil Mickelson has basically vanished from the LIV Golf Tour — and social media. Leading us down a rabbit hole of hidden misconduct claims and exits from three different golf clubs. Not to mention 19 sources, an off-shore oil pipeline and an ex-friend's ex-wife: Ashley Perez.
Read Alan Shipnuck’s reporting at Skratch Golf: https://www.skratch.golf/news/pro-golf/phil-mickelson-misconduct-women-madison-club-bridges-farms-exits
Read Phil Mickelson’s spokesperson’s statement: https://x.com/_davidrumsey/status/2072655169792225420?s=46
Read Skratch’s response: https://x.com/_davidrumsey/status/2072696394997981241?s=46

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 4 RECAP: Dimitrov's Domination, Venus & Serena Return & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick breaks down the biggest stories from Day 4 of Wimbledon 2026, including Grigor Dimitrov's emotional comeback win, Alexander Zverev's growing confidence on grass, Matteo Berrettini's resurgence, and Venus and Serena Williams reuniting in doubles. He also reacts to Taylor Fritz's steady run, the tournament's biggest contenders, and the storylines that could shape the rest of Wimbledon.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 4?
📩 Sign up for the Newsletter & Live Chat w/ us during matches! https://servedmedia.substack.com/
📱If you're new to the Served with Andy Roddick, we're happy you're here! Follow us for more: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast/ https://x.com/Served_Podcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast
Learn more about ServiceNow here: https://www.servicenow.com/?campid=27...
--- --- ---
Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.

entry.title: [BOMANI] The Night Hulk Hogan Shocked the World and Joined the NWO | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Corey Erdman to break down Hulk Hogan’s legendary heel turn at Bash at the Beach 1996, when Hogan shocked fans, joined the NWO, and changed the future of WCW, WWE, and pro wrestling forever.
In this episode of The Right Time, Bomani and Corey revisit Hulkamania, Hollywood Hogan, the rise of the New World Order, and why Hogan’s jump to WCW initially felt like a legacy act before the NWO made him feel brand new again.
They also discuss why the turn hit so hard, how it helped reshape the Monday Night Wars and the Attitude Era, and why Hogan remained the most recognizable star wrestling ever produced.
Topics include Hulk Hogan, NWO, WCW, WWE, Bash at the Beach, Hollywood Hogan, Eric Bischoff, Kevin Nash, Scott Hall, the Monday Night Wars, and wrestling nostalgia.

On Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, we're talking about how the US held off Bosnia and Herzegovina, Folarin Balogun’s red card, and what the plan is for our face-off against Belgium. We’ll take a trip around the rest of the tournament, including Jesse Marsch’s post-game pep talk, Sir Harry Kane is headed to Azteca, Paraguay and Morocco upsetting big European teams on penalties, and England powering past the Congo. Plus we’ll answer your questions in the AT&T Fan Connection!

entry.title: [SGS] Is Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s MSG wedding partly commentary on the nature of celebrity?
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with an episode about all the glimpses they’ve caught so far surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding.
But first, a few things! Serena Williams reportedly injured her knee during her Wimbledon singles match and is doubtful to play doubles with her sister, Venus. World Cupdate: Mexico made history and won the team’s first knockout match in 40 years when they beat Ecuador 2-0.
Then: LeBron is leaving the Lakers! He’s not retiring and he’ll play somewhere else. Will that be the Golden State Warriors with Steph Curry and Draymond Green? The Cleveland Cavaliers? No one knows yet. He did, however, get his son paid by LA before he bounced.
And finally: Taylor and Travis’s wedding! Taylor’s team applied for a street activity permit from July 2nd -July 4th near MSG — is this actually happening? The gals discuss everything we know so far and give their take on why the rollout of info could actually be a brilliant meta-commentary on Taylor’s public image to date.
0:00 The gals say hello 6:18 Serena’s Wimbledon return 9:56 World Cupdate 11:56 LeBron leaving the Lakers 24:26 Fashion mailbag 29:09 Taylor and Travis wedding!
MERCH: https://the-sports-gossip-show.printify.me/ Subscribe to our YouTube channel: ‪@SportsGossipShow‬ Follow on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/sportsgossip Follow on TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/sportsgossipshow Listen on Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-sports-gossip-show/id1769969236 Listen on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/5tvzu1FzPdn8orKIv1OBQR Subscribe to our Substack: https://sportsgossipshow.substack.com/ Partner with us: https://www.sportsgossipshow.com/ Photo Credit: Getty Images Thumbnail Credit: Kate Green/Stacy Revere/Getty Images
entry.content:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with an episode about all the glimpses they’ve caught so far surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding.

But first, a few things! Serena Williams reportedly injured her knee during her Wimbledon singles match and is doubtful to play doubles with her sister, Venus. World Cupdate: Mexico made history and won the team’s first knockout match in 40 years when they beat Ecuador 2-0.

Then: LeBron is leaving the Lakers! He’s not retiring and he’ll play somewhere else. Will that be the Golden State Warriors with Steph Curry and Draymond Green? The Cleveland Cavaliers? No one knows yet. He did, however, get his son paid by LA before he bounced.

And finally: Taylor and Travis’s wedding! Taylor’s team applied for a street activity permit from July 2nd -July 4th near MSG — is this actually happening? The gals discuss everything we know so far and give their take on why the rollout of info could actually be a brilliant meta-commentary on Taylor’s public image to date.

entry.title: [PTFO] The Sporting Class: Not Even FIFA Can Mess This Up | PTFO
entry.summary:
Ex-ESPN president John Skipper was once the most important soccer fan in America. But then he lost the World Cup to FOX — and became a federal witness. So we wanted to know what it’s been like to watch America fall in love with the very tournament Skipper wasn’t allowed to win. And whether federal corruption trials, nine figures worth of commercials and a Trump Tower apartment full of cats can slow the spread of the world’s favorite game.
• Donate to the Kyra Fund, which will support research on Glioblastoma, one of the deadliest cancers: https://fundraise.gbmresearch.org/campaign/812889/donate

entry.title: [CD] Xpeng L03: European Carmakers Will Panic Over This Chinese EV
entry.summary:
The Xpeng Mona L3 is coming for the European EV market. See why mainstream manufacturers are worried about this new competitor.
European car brands like VW and Audi are facing serious competition as premium Chinese EVs enter their home territory. The Xpeng Mona L3 represents a new strategy, bringing advanced, premium tech into the entry-level EV segment. This shift creates a direct challenge for established mainstream OEMs that have previously dominated the region.
In this video, we take a quick first look at the XPeng L03 entry-level EV and break down how XPeng is trying to bring premium-level tech into the mainstream EV segment. This car is clearly aimed at both China and global markets, including Europe.
The bigger picture here is what this car represents. Chinese EV brands are no longer just challenging European luxury cars, they are now targeting the mainstream market where brands like Ford, Nissan, and Renault operate.
Disclaimer: The vehicle shown in the video is the Chinese-spec model filmed in China. Models and configurations for other markets shall be subject to actual vehicles available locally.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ____________________________________________________________ ✅ Highlights:
- Entry-level EV with premium-level technology stack - 3 in-house developed self-driving chips (~2250 TOPS compute) - 800V architecture with fast charging capability - EV and EREV variants with up to 1000km+ combined range

entry.content:
The Xpeng Mona L3 is coming for the European EV market. See why mainstream manufacturers are worried about this new competitor.

European car brands like VW and Audi are facing serious competition as premium Chinese EVs enter their home territory. The Xpeng Mona L3 represents a new strategy, bringing advanced, premium tech into the entry-level EV segment. This shift creates a direct challenge for established mainstream OEMs that have previously dominated the region.

In this video, we take a quick first look at the XPeng L03 entry-level EV and break down how XPeng is trying to bring premium-level tech into the mainstream EV segment. This car is clearly aimed at both China and global markets, including Europe.

The bigger picture here is what this car represents. Chinese EV brands are no longer just challenging European luxury cars, they are now targeting the mainstream market where brands like Ford, Nissan, and Renault operate.

Disclaimer: The vehicle shown in the video is the Chinese-spec model filmed in China. Models and configurations for other markets shall be subject to actual vehicles available locally.

Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
____________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:

- Entry-level EV with premium-level technology stack
- 3 in-house developed self-driving chips (~2250 TOPS compute)
- 800V architecture with fast charging capability
- EV and EREV variants with up to 1000km+ combined range

____________________________________________________________

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 2 RECAP: Serena's Return, Ben Shelton's Collapse & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim recap Wimbledon Day 2, breaking down Serena Williams' long-awaited singles return, Ben Shelton's shocking first-round exit, and what both results mean moving forward. They also discuss Taylor Fritz's title chances, Iga Swiatek's emotional victory, Stan Wawrinka's remarkable longevity, and the biggest storylines unfolding at the All England Club.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 2?
📩 Sign up for the Newsletter & Live Chat w/ us during matches! https://servedmedia.substack.com/

Learn more about ServiceNow here: https://www.servicenow.com/?campid=27...
--- --- ---
Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.

entry.title: [NODUNKS] Raptors bring Kawhi back in shocking trade with Clippers
entry.summary:
Kawhi Leonard has been traded back to the Toronto Raptors in a blockbuster deal with the Los Angeles Clippers. In exchange, the Clippers receive Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, two future first-round picks (2031, 2033), a 2027 first-round pick swap, and two second-round picks. No Dunks debate the risky move and whether this makes the Raps immediate title contenders.

entry.title: [SGS] All the World Cup Round of 32 gossip: Ronaldo’s fiancée falls for AI drama and a Norway hot take
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are thrilled to welcome Hannah Vanbiber to the show to talk all things World Cup for the Round of 32! Hannah is the co-host of the The Athletic FC Daily Cup on Amazon Fire and writer of The Athletic’s daily World Cup newsletter, and an absolute joy to chat with.
The gals and Hannah talk about the craziness of the Paraguay-Germany match, the vibes of the Brazil team, how the USMNT’s chances look against Bosnia and Herzegovina, who’s the most interesting player on the USMNT, the juiciest off-the-field story, and the wildest World Cup story you may not have heard of. Main characters include Erling Haaland, Florian Balogun, Cristiano Ronaldo, Alex Rodriguez, and many more.
Plus: a tease for Thursday’s episode about Taylor and Travis’s wedding!

entry.title: [BOMANI] Why Kevin Durant LEFT the Thunder for the Warriors 10 years ago | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
On this episode of The Right Time with Bomani Jones, Bomani is joined by Logan Murdock to revisit Kevin Durant joining the Golden State Warriors and why KD left the Oklahoma City Thunder for Golden State in 2016. Ten years later, they break down why Kevin Durant chose the Warriors, how the Warriors built the perfect pitch, and why the move became one of the biggest turning points in modern NBA history.
Bomani Jones and Logan Murdock discuss the 2016 NBA offseason, the salary cap spike that made Kevin Durant to the Warriors possible, the Bay Area tech boom, the Warriors culture, and why Golden State felt like the right fit for KD at that moment. They also get into how Steph Curry, Draymond Green, and the Warriors' homegrown core made the team so appealing, plus why the partnership eventually became more complicated behind the scenes.
If you're interested in Kevin Durant highlights, Kevin Durant Warriors history, OKC Thunder history, Golden State Warriors history, NBA free agency, NBA superteams, and basketball analysis from Bomani Jones, this episode breaks it all down.

entry.title: [UFS] Jason Kelce on Messi, USMNT World Cup Hopes & Why Soccer Is Harder Than Football | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Jason Kelce is getting Unfiltered! Landon Donovan and Tim Howard are joined by former Philadelphia Eagles center and podcast host Jason Kelce to discuss his love of soccer. The guys talk about the US Men’s National Team’s World Cup run so far, why USA soccer chants are SO BAD, Messi still being the GOAT at 39, and why youth soccer in America is due for a change.
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim drop every Wednesday & Saturday during this summer’s tournament. entry.title: [PTFO] “Please Enjoy It Or You Are Fired” (feat. Dan Le Batard) | PTFO
entry.summary:
PTFO keeps winning awards. But what do we do for an encore? How do you make something special without losing our standards — or our minds? Do you guys even enjoy this? Pablo visits Miami to see his friend/boss, Dan Le Batard, and Dan’s producer, Chris Cote. They discuss balancing real life; perspective in the face of grief; Oprah; and figuring out how to serve an audience without missing the point.
• Donate to the Kyra Fund, which will support research on Glioblastoma, one of the deadliest cancers: https://fundraise.gbmresearch.org/campaign/812889/donate

entry.title: [SERVED] Jodar’s First Wimbledon, Osaka’s Entrance & More | 2026 Wimbledon Day 1
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick breaks down results from Day 1 of Wimbledon 2026, including Jannik Sinner surviving a gritty five-set battle, João Fonseca's impressive grass-court performance, Coco Gauff and Aryna Sabalenka cruising through, and Naomi Osaka's viral entrance. Roddick also shares the hilarious story of his chaotic first day on ESPN, gives his take on Tommy Paul, Iva Jovic, and other standout performances, and looks ahead at what Day 2 could have in store.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 1?

entry.title: [RTRS] Revisiting The Labaron Philon Pick, Should The Sixers Sign Mitchell Robinson, LaMelo Trade

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
We check in on Mike's well-being and thoughts on the Sixers selection of Labaron Philon in the NBA Draft, and what it means for the franchise moving forward. Then we talk about the LaMelo Ball trade, and finally preview free agency, including whether we'd like a mid-level exception signing of former Knicks center Mitchell Robinson.

entry.title: [UFS] Does USMNT’s Türkiye Loss Matter? Plus Bosnia Knockout Clash & Pochettino’s Plan | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Everyone calm down! Landon Donovan and Tim Howard are unbothered by the United States Men’s National Team’s last-minute loss to Türkiye in the final group stage game of the World Cup. With USMNT having already wrapped up topping the group, head coach Mauricio Pochettino decided to rotate the squad and ultimately lost a tough-fought game against an eliminated Türkiye team.
Landon and Tim give their thoughts on the game, recap the group and preview the USMNT’s upcoming Round of 32 matchup against Bosnia and Herzegovina. The guys also take a trip around the World Cup with Cape Verde advancing in their first-ever tournament appearance – to face Lionel Messi’s Argentina – and other favorite World Cup moments. Plus we’ll answer your questions in the mailbag!
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon are LIVE during this summer’s tournament. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. Send your mailbag questions to feedback@unfilteredsoccer.com (https://www.unfilteredsoccer.com).
#LandonDonovan #TimHoward #USMNT #USSoccer #2026WorldCup #FIFAWorldCup #WorldCup #LionelMessi #CristianoRonaldo #ChristianPulisic
YouTube Chapters 0:00 Intro 2:12 USMNT's loss to Türkiye 11:57 Previewing Bosnia and Herzegovina 17:41 Potential Pochettino extension 23:25 Around the World Cup 25:58 Mbappé and Messi 27:43 Muslera's mistake 30:45 France’s dominance 39:49 Memo Ochoa's moment 43:05 Fan Questions
This episode of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard was recorded at Quad Studios in NYC. The episode was edited, engineered, and mastered by Jeremy Steinkamp. Our producers are Jordan Rizzieri and Andrew Gundling. Our social media lead is Dan Armelli and our social editors are Kyle Curley and Finley Skelton.
Thank you to our sponsor TCL.

entry.title: [PTFO] We Remember the World Cup's Missing Muckraker | PTFO
entry.summary:
He covered soccer before it was cool, promised to go WikiLeaks on FIFA, then got detained and mysteriously collapsed in Qatar. If there was ever someone we needed to hear from, as a global conduit during this corrupt World Cup on American soil, it's Grant Wahl. So his former co-host, Chris Wittyngham — along with his widow and one of the many stars he inspired — helps Pablo Torre listen back to Wahl's lost tapes, re-live his fearless run for office and the rainbow target on his chest… then put the conspiracies to rest.
Previously on PTFO:
• John Green & Daniel Alarcón on World Cup Fandom — and the Worst Messi Interview Ever https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BFgJm0aUhgk
• Tim Weah on World Cup Dreams, the African Diaspora and USMNT's Fair-Weather Knicks Fandom https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_B0SbKYbiKw
• Following the Wasted Money at World Cup 2026 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9J8fYQhfjE0
Plus:
• Watch Chris Wittyngham on CBS Sports Golazo https://www.youtube.com/@cbssportsgolazo
• Subscribe to The Athletic https://theathletic.onelink.me/eNRy/ptfo
entry.content:

entry.title: [UFS] Messi and Ronaldo Are DEFYING Age at the World Cup 😱 | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Two USMNT legends are in awe of what they're witnessing from football's greatest rivals.
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss the incredible World Cup performances of Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo as both stars continue to rewrite soccer history.
Messi and Ronaldo have become the first and second men's players ever to appear on the pitch in six FIFA World Cups. Ronaldo added another historic chapter by scoring twice against Uzbekistan, while Messi celebrated his 39th birthday during the tournament and has already scored five goals in just two World Cup matches.
Howard and Donovan compare the longevity and dominance of Messi and Ronaldo to legendary late-career performances from athletes across other sports, asking a fascinating question:
Are we witnessing the greatest aging athletes in sports history?
Watch as the USMNT icons break down what makes Messi and Ronaldo's sustained excellence so remarkable and why their World Cup performances continue to amaze even former elite players.
⚽ Do you think Messi or Ronaldo's longevity is more impressive?

entry.title: [BOMANI] How LeBron BROKE the 73-Win Warriors in 2016 | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Ethan Strauss to look back at the 2016 NBA Finals and how LeBron James, Kyrie Irving and the Cleveland Cavaliers came back to beat Steph Curry and the 73-win Golden State Warriors.
On this episode of The Right Time, Bomani and Ethan break down what made LeBron’s performance so overwhelming, why Game 7 still feels like one of the biggest legacy games in NBA history, and how the 2016 Finals changed the way people looked at Steph Curry, the Warriors dynasty and the Cavs’ championship in Cleveland.
They discuss LeBron vs. Steph, Kyrie’s role in the comeback, Draymond Green, the Warriors’ collapse, Game 7’s defining plays, and why the 2016 NBA Finals still stands as one of the most important series of the modern era.
Topics include: LeBron James’ control of the 2016 NBA Finals Steph Curry’s legacy after the Warriors blew a 3-1 lead Kyrie Irving’s impact on the Cavs’ championship run The pressure on the 73-win Warriors The biggest moments from Cavs vs. Warriors Game Why Cleveland’s title hit different

entry.title: [SGS] “America’s Sweethearts” reveals the darkest truths about Dallas Cowboys cheerleaders and our culture
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a deep dive of Season 3 of “America's Sweethearts: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders.” The gals talked about Season 2 last year when it came out, which was a very different series that followed the cheerleaders as they fought for better pay.
This year, the antagonist is the outside world, and the show became a show about the show itself.
The gals analyze all of the themes the show presents: Supporting hegemonic masculinity, the Cowboys’ thin, white standard of beauty, the mirage of empowerment, what it means to be famous today, and this season as contextualized by the rise of the tradwife.
Plus: the origins of cheerleading, Reece’s decision to retire, Kelli and Judy leading from a place of fear, the hip surgeries that multiple performers need by the age of 25, the power imbalance that occurs when the show anoints certain women as stars, and much more.
Selected sources from this episode: Sekani Robinson, Black Ballerinas: The Management of Emotional and Aesthetic Labor (2021) La'Tonya Rease Miles, American Beauty: The Cheerleader in American Literature and Popular Culture (2005) Sophie Gilbert, Girl on Girl (2025) Laura Grindstaff, Emily West, Cheerleading and the Gendered Politics of Sport (2006) Alex Kuczynski, Beauty Junkies (2006) Michaele L. Ferguson, Choice Feminism and the Fear of Politics (2010)
0:00 The gals welcome us in 4:05 Fashion mailbag 7:48 America’s Sweethearts deep dive 19:04 Beauty standards on the DCC 28:37 DCC makeover day 38:21 The mirage of empowerment 52:21 Reece’s retirement and impact
