entry.title: [PTFO] John Green & Daniel Alarcón on World Cup Fandom — and the Worst Messi Interview Ever | PTFO
entry.summary:
We are numb, as Americans, to the attention of the globe and the power structures of our sports leagues. But what if love of country could be simple again? And what if fans had more power than we thought? Pablo Torre feels the football osmosis from the (banned) bestselling author and his old friend turned MacArthur Genius, as they offer a liberal-arts education in the beautiful game — from the fault in our superstar interviews to the damage wrought by Alexi Lalas upon our would-be Neymars and Messis. Plus: a pump-up speech that Ted Lasso would never, ever fantasize about.
• Subscribe to "The Away End with Daniel Alarcón and John Green": https://www.youtube.com/@AwayEndPodcast
• Previously on PTFO: John Green on the Fault in Our Health Care — and AFC Wimbledon's Fairytale https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qHiKbkWnjgo
• Subscribe to The Athletic: https://theathletic.onelink.me/eNRy/ptfo

entry.title: [SERVED] Can Wimbledon Ever Be Fast Again? + Tim Henman's London Travel Tips | Q&Andy
entry.summary:
Want to be featured on the next Q&Andy? Send us a video on our socials or email us at askandy@servedmediagroup.com
Andy Roddick sits down with British tennis legend and All England Club board member Tim Henman for a look inside the most iconic tournament in the sport. As the tennis world turns its eyes to Wimbledon, Henman pulls back the curtain on the complex, behind-the-scenes decision-making at Wimbledon—from the controversial wildcard process for top stars and rising prospects to the delicate balance of maintaining 150 years of tradition while modernizing the grounds. The two discuss the reality of the grass-court season, including the impact of surface quality and equipment on the modern game, while reminiscing about their own high-pressure battles on Centre Court against legends like Pete Sampras.
📱 Stay up to date with Q&Andy! Follow us on our socials: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast https://x.com/served_podcast
Thank you to our sponsor: Airbnb

entry.title: [UFS] Ronaldo is BACK, Messi BREAKS RECORD, & Rebecca Lowe Joins the Show | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Messi's breaking records at 39! On Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, we're joined by Rebecca Lowe, FOX's lead studio host for the World Cup to talk Lionel Messi’s incredible run, Mbappe trying to catch him, Ronaldo getting on the score sheet, and England falling apart against Ghana. The guys are addressing Folarin Balogun’s comments about beating Landon’s record, whether or not Mauricio Pochettino should rest USMNT players against Türkiye, and who the US might face in the round of 32. Plus we’ll answer your questions in the AT&T Fan Connection!
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim drop every Wednesday & Saturday during this summer’s tournament. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. entry.content:
Messi's breaking records at 39! On Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, we're joined by Rebecca Lowe, FOX's lead studio host for the World Cup to talk Lionel Messi’s incredible run, Mbappe trying to catch him, Ronaldo getting on the score sheet, and England falling apart against Ghana. The guys are addressing Folarin Balogun’s comments about beating Landon’s record, whether or not Mauricio Pochettino should rest USMNT players against Türkiye, and who the US might face in the round of 32. Plus we’ll answer your questions in the AT&T Fan Connection!

New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim drop every Wednesday & Saturday during this summer’s tournament. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. Send your mailbag questions to feedback@unfilteredsoccer.com (https://www.unfilteredsoccer.com).

entry.title: [SGS] Is Fox Sports purposely playing up the Alexi Lalas and Zlatan Ibrahimović drama?
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a jam-packed episode! First up: For Father’s Day, Gisele Bündchen posted about Joaquim Valente, her new husband and father of her youngest child, but left out her ex-husband Tom Brady. Yikes! Eagles star DeVonta Smith married Mya Danielle and set a new standard for compressed flowers. Former college basketball player Haley Cavinder, on the other hand, married Jake Ferguson of the Cowboys, and it was just standard compression.
The Milwaukee Bucks traded Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Heat, even though the Celtics appear to have offered up Jaylen Brown — which is a little awkward for Jaylen! Also, the gals don’t think this will work for the Heat, sorry to Miami. The Fever’s Myisha Hines-Allen was ejected after a team-wide Fever scuffle against the Mercury, and Caitlin Clark got her fifth tech of the season — there’s a lot of history between Mercury star DeWanna Bonner and the Fever.
Serena Williams is competing in women’s singles at Wimbledon, and former Wimbledon champ & World No. 6 Marketa Vondrousova is suspended from professional tennis until 2030 after refusing to take an anti-doping test in December 2025.
Now, World Cup stuff! Scottish fans are obsessed with Boston and drank all the beer in the city. The Fox Sports drama between Alexi Lalas and Zlatan Ibrahimović is putting host Rebecca Lowe in a very bad position. Norway beat Senegal, thanks to two goals from Erling Haaland, and all the Norwegian fans all pretend to row in the stands! There seem to be some issues in Senegal’s camp — there’s political upheaval in the country, so buckle up for a current events lesson. Curaçao's (playing Ivory Coast Thursday) Eloy Room recorded 15 saves against Ecuador, and Argentina’s Lionel Messi became the highest goalscorer in men's World Cup history on Monday (with France’s Kylian Mbappé close on his heels!).
0:00 The gals say hello 4:28 Gisele’s Father’s Day post 11:55 Compressed flower roundup 22:18 Giannis trade 26:25 Serena returns, Marketa suspended 31:41 World Cup stories

entry.title: [BOMANI] How the 1986 NBA Draft Changed the NBA Forever | Bomani Jones & Howard Bryant

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones and Howard Bryant break down the 1986 NBA Draft and why it remains one of the most important turning points in NBA history. This Time Machine Tuesday episode covers Len Bias, Dennis Rodman, Brad Daugherty, Arvydas Sabonis, the Boston Celtics, the Los Angeles Lakers, and how the 1986 draft changed the league forever.
If you’re looking for NBA history, Len Bias analysis, 1986 NBA Draft context, or a deep dive into the Bird vs. Magic era, this episode goes all the way there. Bomani and Howard Bryant unpack the NBA’s rise in the 1980s, the tragedy of Len Bias, the fallout of the so-called cocaine draft, and why this draft reshaped the future of pro basketball.
Topics include: 1986 NBA Draft Len Bias and the Celtics Dennis Rodman’s draft class Bird, Magic, and the NBA boom Arvydas Sabonis and international basketball How the NBA changed in the late 1980s
#BomaniJones #HowardBryant #NBAHistory #NBADraft #LenBias #DennisRodman #Celtics #Lakers

entry.title: [UFS] Tim Howard Rips Red Cards and DEFENDS Jesse Marsch 🤯 | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
The first red card of the World Cup for mouth-covering has everyone talking after Paraguay's Miguel Almirón was sent off against Tükiye. USMNT legends Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down the controversial decision and they don't agree.
Landon questions the player's actions, while Tim argues the red card was excessive, even calling many yellow and red cards "dumb." The discussion then shifts to Jesse Marsch, one of soccer's most polarizing coaches. Tim comes to Marsch's defense, explaining why he believes the Canada manager's actions and personality are irrelevant and that it's the results that matter.
Do you agree with Tim or Landon? Should Almirón have been sent off? And what's your take on Jesse Marsch?
👇 Sound off in the comments!
#USMNT #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #MiguelAlmiron #Paraguay #JesseMarsch #CanadaSoccer #WorldCup #SoccerNews #FootballNews #SoccerDebate #WorldCup2026 #FIFA

entry.title: [PTFO] Sarah Jessica Parker on the Power of Sports and the City | PTFO
entry.summary:
New York is palpably horny, right now. And happy. So we invited the patron saint of sex and this town to chase euphoria, appreciate the romance of the American idea, advise these young Knicks, remember the halcyon days of HBO... and make the case for reading books in the crowd.

entry.title: [UFS] Can the USMNT Actually Win the World Cup? Tim Howard & Landon Donovan Debate | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
The US have won their FIFA World Cup Group! On Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, our hosts break down the U.S.’s win over Australia including Alex Freeman’s first World Cup goal. Plus Paraguay holds Türkiye off while down a man, Neymar to return soon from injury as Brazil look to lock up their group, and Canada absolutely dominate Qatar. And Tim and Landon are taking YOUR World Cup questions in our mailbag.
#FIFAWorldCup #WorldCup2026 #AlexFreeman #RicardoPepi #FolarinBalogun #USMNT #Paraguay #Turkiye #Brazil #Mexico #SouthKorea #Canada #JesseMarsch #JonathanDavid #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #UnfilteredSoccer #Soccer #Football #WorldCup #MiguelAlmiron #JesseMarsch #ChristianPulisic #WestonMcKennie #SerginoDest #Neymar #Raphinha
YouTube Chapters: 0:00 Intro 1:41 USMNT vs. Australia recap 11:52 Seattle vs. LA atmosphere 17:46 Pulisic's injury 23:49 USMNT 2-0 29:47 Almirón receives red for covering mouth 35:06 Around the World Cup 47:25 Fan Questions 59:44 Can the USMNT win the World Cup?
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim drop every Tuesday. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. Send your mailbag questions to feedback@unfilteredsoccer.com (https://www.unfilteredsoccer.com).
Thank you to today's sponsor:
TCL. Find the ultimate 98" TV experience at TCL.com.
This episode of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard was edited and mastered by Jeremy Steinkamp, with engineering support from Adam Braunstein. Our producers are Jordan Rizzieri and Andrew Gundling. Our social media lead is Dan Armelli and our social editors are Kyle Curley and Finley Skelton.

entry.title: [NODUNKS] Who are the top NBA free agents this offseason?
entry.summary:
The 2026 NBA free-agency period begins at the end of June as teams start negotiating with players before official announcements are made July 6. No Dunks take a look at the 12 or so best players (potentially) available on the open market, including restricted free agents, unrestricted free agents, and those with player options and team options.

entry.title: [PTFO] Tim Weah on World Cup Dreams, the African Diaspora and USMNT's Fair-Weather Knicks Fandom | PTFO
entry.summary:
Timothy Weah is the realest member of Team USA — an activist athlete, from Brooklyn and the African diaspora, choosing to represent a divisive nation as it tries bringing the globe a little closer together. In a rare sit-down ahead of the U.S. men's national team match against Australia, he explains the pressure of this moment, the power of soccer over politics... and why the Knicks' good-luck charm might need to strike again.
• Watch The Athletic FC Podcast: https://www.youtube.com/@TheAthleticFCPodcast
• Watch Tifo Football by The Athletic: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCGYYNGmyhZ_kwBF_lqqXdAQ
• Subscribe to The Athletic: https://theathletic.onelink.me/eNRy/ptfo
Visit Boost Mobile. Unlock your phone. #boostmobilepartner @boostmobile

entry.title: [UFS] Messi Dominates While Lamine Yamal Makes His World Cup Debut 👀

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Lionel Messi continues to prove why he's one of the greatest players in soccer history, while Lamine Yamal officially announced himself on the World Cup stage. 🌎⚽
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down Argentina and Spain's opening matches at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, highlighting Messi's brilliance and discussing what made Yamal's highly anticipated World Cup debut so special.
From the GOAT's continued excellence to the rise of one of soccer's brightest young stars, this episode covers two of the biggest storylines of the tournament so far.
Can Messi lead Argentina to another World Cup title? And how far can Lamine Yamal take Spain?

entry.title: [UFS] USMNT's Biggest Question Before Australia: Will Christian Pulisic Play? | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Christian Pulisic's status could have a major impact on the USMNT's biggest World Cup match yet.
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down the latest on Pulisic's availability and preview a crucial showdown between the United States and Australia at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Can the USMNT continue its strong start, or will the Socceroos provide their toughest test of the tournament so far? The guys discuss key matchups, expectations, and what the U.S. needs to do to secure another massive result.

entry.title: [BOMANI] The Brendan Sorsby Case EXPOSES College Football’s Biggest Problem | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Joel Anderson for a wide-ranging conversation about the Brendan Sorsby gambling case, the NCAA’s response, and what it says about the future of college sports. Bomani and Joel break down why college football’s reaction to Sorsby felt so extreme, how the NCAA continues to struggle in court, and why the Protect College Sports Act could be a major problem for athletes.
They also discuss whether Sorsby could end up in the NFL Supplemental Draft, how teams might evaluate him, and why gambling addiction should be treated more seriously as sports betting becomes impossible to avoid. Later, Bomani and Joel get into the hypocrisy around punishing young athletes, and Joel’s ongoing battle with arguing on the internet.

entry.title: [SGS] Why the Berhalter-Reyna family feud was one of the most dramatic scandals in USMNT history
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with everything you want to know about the USMNT players as you watch them. Who are they dating? What club do they play for? Where are they from? Info on Christian Pulisic, Tim Weah, Tim Ream, Weston Mckennie, Florian Balogun, Alex Freeman, and more.
And then: A deep dive into the feud between two royal American families of soccer. Seb Berhalter and Gio Reyna both play for the USMNT, but did you know that their families used to be super close? Until Gregg became Gio’s coach and Gio’s parents, Claudio and Danielle, got involved, that is. You don’t want to miss this one!

entry.title: [UFS] Messi, Mbappé & Haaland Shine in a Wild FIFA World Cup | Unfiltered Soccer LIVE

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Lionel Messi is already making history at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down Messi's brilliant performance for Argentina against Algeria, Erling Haaland's long-awaited World Cup debut for Norway, and Kylian Mbappé's standout showing in France's win over Senegal.
The guys also preview a crucial USMNT showdown with Australia, discuss the biggest storylines from around the tournament, and answer fan questions in the AT&T Fan Connection.
From Messi and Mbappé to Haaland and the USMNT, we've got everything you need to know from the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entry.title: [BOMANI] Victor Wembanyama’s heel turn, Jalen Brunson’s New York Takeover | Bomani Jones & Domonique Foxworth

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Dominique Foxworth for another episode of The Right Time, and they start with the massive NBA Finals ratings after the Knicks’ championship run. Bomani and Dominique discuss why New York’s title felt so big, how Jalen Brunson became a permanent New York legend, and why the biggest winner of the Knicks’ championship might actually be James Dolan.
Then, Bomani and Dominique get into the backlash around Victor Wembanyama after the Finals. Was Wemby really a “villain,” or are fans just looking for the next superstar to tear down? They break down the criticism of his physicality, the handshake discourse, and why the reaction to Wemby says a lot about how we talk about young stars.
Plus, Bomani shares what it was like watching Senegal vs. France in Harlem during the World Cup, the energy around soccer in New York City, and why the World Cup hits differently depending on which neighborhood you’re in.
Topics include: Knicks championship reaction, Jalen Brunson’s New York status, James Dolan’s redemption moment, Victor Wembanyama backlash, NBA Finals ratings, handshake discourse, World Cup energy in NYC, Senegal vs. France, and why the Nets may never catch the Knicks.
Chapters: 0:00 Intro & Dominique Foxworth joins the show 1:15 NBA Finals ratings boom and the Knicks effect 9:00 Victor Wembanyama's Finals performance gets reassessed 19:00 The Anthony Edwards handshake controversy 26:20 Jalen Brunson's rise to New York legend status 33:50 The biggest winner of the Knicks title: James Dolan 40:10 Did the Sphere accidentally save the Knicks? 47:45 Soccer crowds, fan culture, and demographics 52:00 What happens to the Nets now? 55:20 Outro . . . entry.title: [PTFO] Celebrating These Knicks Championship Vibes with Desus Nice
entry.summary:
Transcendence on the court. Dancing in the streets. And a drunk Instagram Live for the ages. Why wait 'til the great Knicks parade of 2026? Pablo raises a glass (while devouring an egg-and-cheese) with Desus — his fellow New Yorker and bard of Knicks nation — to the commute of a champion and the beauty of a bandwagon; to the drip of Landry Shamet and the sweat of Patrick Ewing; to KAT and OG and Wu-Tang and, yes, even Coldplay. Just not that traitor from Sesame Street.

entry.title: [SGS] World Cup gossip: Cape Verde’s 40-year-old goalie, Iran’s complications, and Tunisia’s new hot coach
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back, reporting from a city still buzzing after the Knicks emerged victorious from the Finals. The boys are being silly on TV, KAT shouted out Jordyn Woods and her lucky bag, the gals decide that Mikal Bridges is the only player allowed to go live, and the parade is set for Thursday.
For all you hockey fans out there: The Carolina Hurricanes took the Stanley Cup with a winning combo of a nerd in the front office and a jock leading the charge on the ice. If you don’t know what that means, listen to the show!
Then, the gals dive into the World Cup, which is already sucking them in with the never-ending intrigue. Cape Verde is the story of the Group Stage so far, with its 40-year-old goalie, Vozinha, who saved the day in a draw against heavily favored Spain. Plus, one of Cape Verde’s players was invited to join the team via LinkedIn. The US beat Paraguay on Friday, and the gals will have a deep dive episode into the USMNT on Thursday. Tunisia fired their manager after the first game and hired a notoriously hot French coach, Herve Renard.
And finally: Some details and info about the Iranian national team’s very strange and complicated situation, and why their best player was left off the roster.
0:00 The gals say hello 0:18 The Knicks are NBA Champions 16:55 Hurricanes win Stanley Cup 24:07 World Cup highlights
MERCH: https://the-sports-gossip-show.printify.me/ Subscribe to our YouTube channel: ‪@SportsGossipShow‬ Follow on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/sportsgossip Follow on TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/sportsgossipshow Listen on Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-sports-gossip-show/id1769969236 Listen on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/5tvzu1FzPdn8orKIv1OBQR Subscribe to our Substack: https://sportsgossipshow.substack.com/ Partner with us: https://www.sportsgossipshow.com/ Photo Credit: Getty Images Thumbnail Credit: Jamie Squire/Florencia Tan Jun/William West/Getty Images
entry.content:
Madeline and Charlotte are back, reporting from a city still buzzing after the Knicks emerged victorious from the Finals. The boys are being silly on TV, KAT shouted out Jordyn Woods and her lucky bag, the gals decide that Mikal Bridges is the only player allowed to go live, and the parade is set for Thursday.

For all you hockey fans out there: The Carolina Hurricanes took the Stanley Cup with a winning combo of a nerd in the front office and a jock leading the charge on the ice. If you don’t know what that means, listen to the show!

Then, the gals dive into the World Cup, which is already sucking them in with the never-ending intrigue. Cape Verde is the story of the Group Stage so far, with its 40-year-old goalie, Vozinha, who saved the day in a draw against heavily favored Spain. Plus, one of Cape Verde’s players was invited to join the team via LinkedIn. The US beat Paraguay on Friday, and the gals will have a deep dive episode into the USMNT on Thursday. Tunisia fired their manager after the first game and hired a notoriously hot French coach, Herve Renard.

And finally: Some details and info about the Iranian national team’s very strange and complicated situation, and why their best player was left off the roster.

0:00 The gals say hello
0:18 The Knicks are NBA Champions
16:55 Hurricanes win Stanley Cup
24:07 World Cup highlights

entry.title: [BOMANI] Maradona’s Hand of God, Zidane’s Headbutt & the World Cup’s Wildest Moments | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Spencer Hall for a Time Machine Tuesday edition of The Right Time to revisit some of the most chaotic, controversial, and unforgettable WTF moments in World Cup history.
Bo and Spencer break down Diego Maradona’s infamous “Hand of God” goal against England in the 1986 World Cup, the geopolitical weight behind Argentina vs. England, and why Maradona followed one of soccer’s most blatant acts of cheating with one of the greatest goals the sport has ever seen.
Then, they jump to the 2006 World Cup Final and Zinedine Zidane’s stunning headbutt against Italy, discussing how trash talk, pressure, and Italian defensive dark arts helped create one of the most shocking exits in sports history.
Plus, Bo and Spencer look ahead to the 2026 World Cup in North America, from brutal summer heat to public transportation problems, stadium naming chaos, and why international fans may not be ready for how big — and inconvenient — the United States really is.

entry.title: [BOMANI] Jalen Brunson, the Knicks, and the NBA Championship That Took Over New York | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
The Knicks finally did it.
Bomani Jones reacts to the New York Knicks winning the NBA championship, Jalen Brunson’s legendary 45-point closeout performance, and what this title means for New York City. Bo breaks down how Brunson cemented himself as the king of New York, why the Knicks’ depth, conditioning, and maturity ultimately separated them from the Spurs, and how Mike Brown coached a championship-level series.
Bomani also reflects on the unique relationship between the Knicks and New York City — from Madison Square Garden to the streets, bars, parks, and neighborhoods that turned this championship run into a citywide experience. Why does this title feel different from other championships? Why do the Knicks hit New Yorkers so deeply? And what does this moment say about basketball as theater, culture, and community?
Plus, Bomani discusses the start of the World Cup, the excitement around the U.S. men’s national team, and the experience of international fans discovering America through sports, road trips, Waffle House, Buc-ee’s, and the South.
00:00 Introduction 01:30 The Knicks incredible title run 20:00 What made this title so special 38:00 USA wins first World Cup Match Subscribe to Supercast for Ad-Free Episodes: https://righttime.supercast.com/

entry.title: [SGS] The Knicks’ insane Game 4 comeback is a model for all leaders
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a TedTalk — sorry, episode — about the why the Knicks are modeling great leadership and how the team is winning despite a ton of distractions. The gals talk about what it was like to be in the building for the insane Game 4 comeback, the celebrities they saw, and the passion of fans who couldn’t be in the building.
Then they go on a deep dive into how the Knicks are exhibiting the psychological traits needed to win it all. From Karl-Anthony Towns and Jose Alvarado’s press conference, to Jalen Brunson’s ability to believe, to OG Anunoby’s incredible game-winner, to coach Mike Brown’s embracing of adversity, New York is behaving like a true team with great leadership.
They can visualize success, and they should be a lesson for everyone who is in charge of (or wants to make a meaningful difference within) an organization.
0:00 The gals serenade us 00:59 NBA Finals Game 3 reactions 19:07 Fashion mailbag 22:51 Deep dive into the Knicks’ success

entry.title: [BOMANI] Wemby, De’Aaron Fox and the Spurs Blew an All-Time Lead vs. Knicks | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones reacts to one of the wildest NBA playoff endings in recent memory as the New York Knicks storm back against Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs. After the Spurs dominated the first half and looked ready to take control of the series, everything fell apart late — from rushed shots and questionable coaching decisions to De’Aaron Fox’s brutal second-half execution.
Bomani breaks down why this collapse reminded him of infamous football meltdowns like the Houston Oilers blowing a 35-3 lead and the Atlanta Falcons’ 28-3 Super Bowl disaster. He explains why San Antonio’s inability to slow the game down, manage the clock, and protect a massive lead turned a dominant performance into an all-time choke.
The conversation also gets into Wemby’s missed free throws, OG Anunoby’s incredible game-winning tip-in, Jalen Brunson’s late-game shot-making, Josh Hart nearly becoming the story for the wrong reason, and why Madison Square Garden’s nervous energy makes Knicks playoff games feel different. Bomani also questions whether the Knicks’ comeback was more about their resilience or San Antonio completely losing control.
Plus, Bomani discusses the NBA’s ongoing officiating discourse, why ref talk takes over every playoff conversation, how social media fuels conspiracy theories, and what to expect in Game 5 as the Spurs try to avoid letting one loss beat them twice.

entry.title: [UFS] What Happens If the USMNT Loses Pochettino? | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
What does U.S. Soccer look for in a head coach?
With reports linking USMNT head coach Mauricio Pochettino to AC Milan after the World Cup, questions about the future of the position continue to surface. U.S. Soccer CEO and Secretary General JT Batson joins Landon Donovan and Tim Howard to discuss what the federation values in a head coach and how leadership shapes the direction of the program.
The conversation also explores the evolution of soccer in the United States and where the sport is headed in the years ahead.

entry.title: [BOMANI] Victor Wembanyama's Villain Arc + Knicks have a HUGE problem in the NBA Finals | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
On this episode of The Right Time, Bomani Jones is joined by Tom Haberstroh to break down why Victor Wembanyama has completely changed this series and why the New York Knicks suddenly look like they’re running out of answers. They dig into Wemby’s dominance on both ends, how San Antonio keeps getting him downhill and at the rim, and why Tom thinks the Spurs should actually feel like they could be up 3-0 right now.
Bomani and Tom also get into what’s going wrong for the Knicks late in games, including Jalen Brunson’s extreme ball dominance, the lack of flow in New York’s offense, and Karl-Anthony Towns disappearing in crunch time after such a strong start to the series. From there, they discuss whether the Knicks can make the adjustments needed in Game 4, whether Wemby has already become the biggest force in the series, and why his physical edge and swagger are turning him into one of the most polarizing stars in the league.
They also hit the controversial officiating around Wemby’s contact with Brunson, the NBA’s response to that play, what this matchup says about who’s built for the moment, and even where Jalen Brunson belongs in the all-time Knicks conversation.

entry.title: [SGS] Trump messed up the vibes at Knicks-Spurs Game 3: A behind-the-scenes recap of the NBA Finals
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a normal episode from Brooklyn after a whirlwind trip to San Antonio and Madison Square Garden for Games 1 and 3 of the NBA Finals. They talk about what it was like staying at the same hotel as all of ESPN’s major broadcasters in Texas, the state of vibes in San Antonio, and how crazy New York City was after the Game 2 win on Saturday.
Then, the gals take listeners behind the scenes of Game 3, which was the first Finals game at Madison Square Garden since 1999. The whole operation was messed up thanks to Donald Trump. He decided he was going to show up, after being invited by Knicks owner James Dolan, and make everyone’s lives miserable by causing a massive disruption to Manhattan and at the Garden.
The vibes were definitely skewed by his presence, but the Knicks did their best to fight them off. The Spurs ultimately won — is this a harbinger of things to come? The gals take Shareholders to the Garden for a play-by-play of the most memorable night in New York in decades.
0:00 The gals are back home! 2:44 Stories from San Antonio 17:20 Game 3 Recap 37:49 Predictions for the rest of the finals

entry.content:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a normal episode from Brooklyn after a whirlwind trip to San Antonio and Madison Square Garden for Games 1 and 3 of the NBA Finals. They talk about what it was like staying at the same hotel as all of ESPN’s major broadcasters in Texas, the state of vibes in San Antonio, and how crazy New York City was after the Game 2 win on Saturday.

Then, the gals take listeners behind the scenes of Game 3, which was the first Finals game at Madison Square Garden since 1999. The whole operation was messed up thanks to Donald Trump. He decided he was going to show up, after being invited by Knicks owner James Dolan, and make everyone’s lives miserable by causing a massive disruption to Manhattan and at the Garden.

The vibes were definitely skewed by his presence, but the Knicks did their best to fight them off. The Spurs ultimately won — is this a harbinger of things to come? The gals take Shareholders to the Garden for a play-by-play of the most memorable night in New York in decades.

0:00 The gals are back home!
2:44 Stories from San Antonio
17:20 Game 3 Recap
37:49 Predictions for the rest of the finals

entry.title: [BOMANI] Victor Wembanyama Has the Knicks SHOOK + Brendan Sorsby’s NCAA Gambling Case | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones reacts to Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs flipping the NBA Finals with a physical Game 3 win over the Knicks, including why New York looked rattled, why Jalen Brunson got baited into ISO ball, and why Wemby set the tone in a hostile environment.
Later, Bomani breaks down Brendan Sorsby’s NCAA gambling case, the temporary injunction that let him play this season, and why the bigger story is about zero-tolerance policies, addiction, and the NCAA’s athlete-first standard.
Plus, Bomani gets to a few voicemails and stories on Ray Allen, Scott Skiles, and White Keisha.

entry.title: [UFS] The 2026 USMNT World Cup Preview | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
The road to the 2026 World Cup starts here.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard preview the 2026 FIFA World Cup in North America and break down where the USMNT stands as the team wraps up its pre-tournament friendlies.
The guys are also joined by U.S. Soccer CEO and Secretary General JT Batson for a conversation about the tournament, the future of the sport in America, and what comes next after the World Cup. Plus, in the AT&T Mailbag, Landon and Tim discuss fan interactions and share what supporters should know as World Cup action gets underway.

entry.title: [BOMANI] Knicks come home for game 3, Victor Wembanyama Backlash, World Cup Fever heats up | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones breaks down why the New York Knicks suddenly look like a completely different team in the NBA Finals, why they feel inevitable with a 2-0 lead, and why this run is starting to feel a lot bigger than anyone expected.
Bo also gives Karl-Anthony Towns his proper credit, arguing that KAT has been the best player on the floor, while pushing back on the easy Victor Wembanyama criticism by explaining how much of his rough night was really about the Knicks’ size, physicality, and defense.
From there, Bo and Ryan get into the huge NBA Finals ratings bump, what Victor’s star power means for the league, why the World Cup’s dangerous heat is becoming one of the tournament’s biggest storylines, and why Anthropic’s warning about powerful AI sounds a lot less crazy than people want to admit.

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Arsenal, PSG, and the state of the Premier League.
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard dive into Arsenal's Champions League Final loss to PSG, with Donovan sharing his perspective on the result and the pair debating Arsenal's style of play.
The conversation also turns to Liverpool, as the former USMNT teammates discuss Arne Slot's departure and what it could mean for one of England's biggest clubs.
From European soccer to Premier League storylines, Donovan and Howard break down some of the biggest topics in the game.

entry.title: [UFS] Christian Pulisic's Defining World Cup Moment vs Iran | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Reliving one of the biggest goals in modern USMNT history.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard look back on Christian Pulisic's decisive goal against Iran at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.
With the United States needing a win to advance after draws against Wales and England, the pressure could not have been higher. Donovan and Howard revisit the match, the atmosphere, and the moment that sent the USMNT into the knockout stage.
The episode also features a look back at the road to that match and a trivia challenge centered around one of the most memorable nights in recent U.S. Soccer history.

entry.content:
Reliving one of the biggest goals in modern USMNT history.

As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard look back on Christian Pulisic's decisive goal against Iran at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

With the United States needing a win to advance after draws against Wales and England, the pressure could not have been higher. Donovan and Howard revisit the match, the atmosphere, and the moment that sent the USMNT into the knockout stage.

The episode also features a look back at the road to that match and a trivia challenge centered around one of the most memorable nights in recent U.S. Soccer history.

#USMNT #ChristianPulisic #WorldCup2022 #IranVsUSA #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #USSoccer #AmericanSoccer #WorldCup #SoccerHistory #USMNTFans #SoccerTalk #FootballTalk #WorldCupMoments #RoadTo2026

New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim drop every Tuesday. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. Send your mailbag questions to feedback@unfilteredsoccer.com (https://www.unfilteredsoccer.com).

This bonus content is brought to you by Jim Beam.

Jim Beam is the Official Spirit of U.S. Soccer. Whether you’re following from the stadium or from home, grab a Jim Beam & Lemonade and enjoy with those around you. Visit https://www.jimbeam.com/en-us/cocktails/beam-and-lemonade.

This Unfiltered Soccer bonus content with Landon Donovan and Tim Howard was edited and mastered by Collin Kushner with engineering support from Eric Newell and Jeremy Steinkamp. Our producers are Andrew Gundling and Jordan Rizzieri. Our social media lead is Dan Armelli and our social editors are Kyle Curley and Finley Skelton.

entry.title: [SGS] The ultimate Knicks-Spurs Finals preview with DeAndre Jordan, Monty McCutchen, and Will Reeve
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with Part II of their NBA Finals extravaganza! The Sports Gossip Show was honored to be one of nine podcasts that the NBA invited to their first-ever Podcast Row. This episode is the second of two shows made up of interviews the gals conducted against the backdrop of the Spurs’ arena before Game 1.
First up is DeAndre Jordan, current NBA player, NBA All Star, and Finals champion. He talked about what it takes to be a good teammate, the wildest prank that was ever pulled on him in a locker room, and what it feels like to be traded and then have to play a game immediately with your new team. Then, a conversation with Head of Referee Training & Development, Monty McCutchen, who tells the gals how refs are chosen for the Finals, and much more. Finally, the gals talk to Will Reeve, who is covering the Finals for Good Morning America, about the spirit in New York City when the Knicks are this good.
A huge thank you to the NBA for making this possible!
0:00 The gals are back from the Finals! 3:08 DeAndre Jordan 15:51 Monty McCutchen 30:21 Fashion mailbag 32:16 Will Reeve

entry.title: [UFS] Matt Freese or Matt Turner: Who Should Start for the USMNT? | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Who should be the USMNT's No. 1 goalkeeper at the World Cup?
The competition for the United States' starting goalkeeper spot remains one of the biggest storylines heading into the FIFA World Cup. With Matt Freese and Matt Turner both in the conversation, Tim Howard and Landon Donovan break down the goalkeeper rotation and what it could mean for Mauricio Pochettino's squad.
The discussion also covers Christian Pulisic ending his goalless streak, the USMNT's performance against Senegal, and the key takeaways as the team prepares for the tournament. From selection decisions to standout performances, Donovan and Howard analyze where the USMNT stands heading into the World Cup.

entry.title: [PTFO] "This Is Mind-Blowing": Steve Ballmer's Aspiration I.O.U., Uncovered | PTFO
entry.summary:
As Adam Silver calls for the NBA's Kawhi Leonard probe to wrap up and Aspiration co-founder Joe Sanberg gets 14 years behind bars, Pablo Torre investigates how Clippers owner Steve Ballmer covered his tracks on a side deal of his own — and with the SEC on the case. Former DOJ prosecutor Lou Manzo returns to make sense of the relationship between a billionaire and a fraudster, while David Samson is finally convinced that the richest owner in sports has lost credibility.
(Additional reporting by Anita Bennett)

entry.title: [BOMANI] Knicks DOMINATE Spurs in game 1 of NBA Finals, Russell Wilson's Hall of Fame case | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
In this episode of The Right Time, Bomani Jones and Danny Parkins break down why Karl-Anthony Towns might be the real key to a New York Knicks title run.
Bomani argues that KAT has been the Knicks' best player in the postseason and explains why New York looks its most dangerous when the offense runs through Towns instead of falling into heavy Jalen Brunson isolation ball.
They also get into how Towns' aggressiveness has changed the way people see him, why his effort level has been the biggest difference, and what this kind of playoff run could mean for his long-term legacy if the Knicks finish the job.
Later in the episode, Bomani and Danny debate Russell Wilson's Hall of Fame case, including how much his Pro Bowls matter, how his resume compares to Matthew Stafford, and whether public opinion has swung too far against him after the Denver years.

entry.title: [PTFO] Hot on the Trail of Jeffrey Epstein with Reporter Julie K. Brown | PTFO
entry.summary:
Julie K. Brown went from working in a bell factory to ringing the alarm on masters of the universe and (finally) winning a Pulitzer Prize. The Miami Herald investigative reporter visits Pablo Torre to shed light on the culture of silence that protected Jeffrey Epstein for so long, the legal dream team that struck his secret deal, then how Epstein pulled out all the stops... to stop her reporting. Plus: the whistleblower who gave away the A-Rod steroid scandal, sneaking into a hospital to interview Joe Frazier... and the maddening therapy of Philly sports.
• Take the PTFO audience survey: https://forms.gle/i2K6FecAbvBqDxRK9
• Read "Perversion of Justice" by Julie K. Brown: https://www.harpercollins.com/products/perversion-of-justice-julie-k-brown?variant=44010796908578
Previously on PTFO:
• Part 1: Investigating Harvard's Hidden Epstein Files — WTF Is the Epstein Fund for Women's Sports? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1JclWYNwHQ
• Part 2: New Files Reveal How Epstein Paid Harvard to Launder His Image https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lmOHrXq2jpM
• We Showed a Basketball Icon His Name in the Epstein Files. Things Got Awkward. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Le4j0Goj1NI

entry.title: [BOMANI] Wemby arrives at the NBA Finals, Jay-Z vs. Drake, A.J. Brown & Patriots pressure | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones and Jason Goff break down the NBA Finals matchup between the Spurs and Knicks, with a deep dive into Victor Wembanyama, Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns, and the biggest storylines shaping this series. From Wemby’s rise on the biggest stage in basketball to the pressure on Brunson and KAT to deliver in the Finals, this episode covers what matters most heading into a massive championship matchup.
They also get into whether Victor Wembanyama is on a path we haven’t seen since LeBron, why the Knicks’ guards could decide the series, and why these Finals feel loaded with legacy-defining stakes. Beyond the NBA Finals talk, Bomani and Jason also discuss the possibility of a Jay-Z vs. Drake battle, why that rap beef feels unwinnable for Drake, what A.J. Brown could mean for the Patriots, and how much pressure could be coming with that move.

entry.content:
Bomani Jones and Jason Goff break down the NBA Finals matchup between the Spurs and Knicks, with a deep dive into Victor Wembanyama, Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns, and the biggest storylines shaping this series. From Wemby’s rise on the biggest stage in basketball to the pressure on Brunson and KAT to deliver in the Finals, this episode covers what matters most heading into a massive championship matchup.

They also get into whether Victor Wembanyama is on a path we haven’t seen since LeBron, why the Knicks’ guards could decide the series, and why these Finals feel loaded with legacy-defining stakes. Beyond the NBA Finals talk, Bomani and Jason also discuss the possibility of a Jay-Z vs. Drake battle, why that rap beef feels unwinnable for Drake, what A.J. Brown could mean for the Patriots, and how much pressure could be coming with that move.

entry.title: [UFS] Pulisic Scores, USMNT Goalkeeper Debate & Arsenal's Champions League Collapse | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
The World Cup is about to get Unfiltered.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down the USMNT's match against Senegal, including Christian Pulisic ending his goal drought and the ongoing competition for the United States' No. 1 goalkeeper spot.
The conversation also turns to Mauricio Pochettino, recent reports surrounding his future, and what it could mean for the USMNT heading into the FIFA World Cup.
Away from international soccer, the guys discuss PSG's Champions League victory over Arsenal and debate where Arsenal stand among Europe's elite clubs.
Plus, in the AT&T Mailbag, Landon and Tim answer questions about jersey numbers and Diego Luna being left off the USMNT World Cup roster.

entry.title: [SGS] The ultimate Knicks-Spurs Finals preview with Gary Payton, Malika Andrews, and more
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with one of the most exciting episodes yet before the NBA Finals kick off! The Sports Gossip Show was honored to be one of nine podcasts that the NBA invited to their first-ever Podcast Row. This episode is the first of two shows made up of observations from the Finals — and then interviews the gals conducted against the backdrop of the Spurs’ arena before Game 1.
The first guest is ABC/ESPN broadcaster Malika Andrews, who talks about sitting down with Karl-Anthony Towns, Jalen Brunson, and Victor Wembenyama, what it takes to work on the national stage, and how she decides who she thinks will win it all. Then, the gals talk to two dear friends of the show from The Athletic, Fred Katz and Jared Weiss. Fred spills some tea from his forthcoming book about the resurgence of the Knicks, and Jared talks about getting to know Wembenyama and the Spurs in his year covering the team. Fred also tells an unhinged story about Josh Hart and the candy Mike & Ikes.
Then, last but certainly not least, the gals sit down with NBA legend Gary Payton. He talks about why he won’t retire his number for anyone but the Seattle Supersonics, what it’s like having his son play in the league, and then he completes an absolutely ridiculous task that Madeline and Charlotte throw his way.
You don’t want to miss this one. The gals want to send a huge thanks to the NBA for this opportunity — and stayed tuned for Part II!
0:00 The gals have a serious message 1:45 We’re at Podcast Row at the NBA Finals! 10:33 ESPN’s Malika Andrews 31:02 The Athletic’s Fred Katz and Jared Weiss 49:10 NBA legend Gary Payton
MERCH: https://the-sports-gossip-show.printify.me/ Subscribe to our YouTube channel: ‪@SportsGossipShow‬ Follow on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/sportsgossip Follow on TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/sportsgossipshow Listen on Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-sports-gossip-show/id1769969236 Listen on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/5tvzu1FzPdn8orKIv1OBQR Subscribe to our Substack: https://sportsgossipshow.substack.com/ Partner with us: https://www.sportsgossipshow.com/ Photo Credit: Getty Images

entry.title: [BOMANI] Why Dirk Nowitzki’s 2011 Title Run Changed Everything about his Legacy | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones and Vinnie Goodwill go back to the two NBA Finals that defined Dirk Nowitzki’s career: 2006 and 2011.
They break down how Dirk changed the league, why the “soft” label followed him for years, how the We Believe Warriors loss stuck to him, and why that 2011 championship run completely changed the way we talk about his legacy.
If you remember Dwyane Wade living at the line, the fake cough, the Heatles pressure cooker, and Dallas finally getting its revenge, this is the full Dirk story.

entry.title: [UFS] The Night Tim Howard Made World Cup History | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Reliving one of the greatest performances in World Cup history.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard revisit one of the most memorable matches in U.S. Soccer history: the United States' Round of 16 showdown with Belgium at the 2014 World Cup.
After advancing from a difficult group, the USMNT faced a Belgian side loaded with talent. Over the course of the match, Tim Howard delivered a record-setting performance between the posts, making more saves than any goalkeeper had ever recorded in a World Cup match.
Donovan and Howard look back on the match, the atmosphere, the challenges posed by Belgium, and the performance that cemented Howard's place in World Cup history.

entry.title: [UFS] Clint Dempsey & Bob Bradley Get Candid on U.S. Soccer | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Clint Dempsey reflects on his rise with the USMNT.
In a candid conversation with former teammates Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, Clint Dempsey looks back on his journey with the United States Men's National Team and some of the defining moments of his international career.
The discussion includes Dempsey's experience during the 2009 FIFA Confederations Cup and a memorable conversation with former USMNT head coach Bob Bradley, offering insight into a pivotal period for the program.
From leadership and competition to the growth of American soccer, the episode revisits one of the most influential eras in USMNT history.

entry.title: [SGS] Why NHL fans hate the Vegas Golden Knights with DJ Bean
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are thrilled to be joined by DJ Bean of the daily show “What Chaos” to talk about the NHL Playoffs and Stanley Cup Final, as many of the Shareholders have been asking for!
But first: A couple updates from the NBA Finals. The Oklahoma City Thunder are up on the Spurs, 3-2, and the winner of the series will face the New York Knicks. The Cleveland Cavaliers are keeping Kenny Atkinson as their coach and are re-signing James Harden to a multi-year deal. Huh! Trump may go to the NBA Finals in New York, and the transportation situation at Madison Square Garden is going to be very interesting if the Knicks have a home game and the World Cup starts on the same day.
Then: Stanley Cup Final stuff! DJ joins to talk about why fans hate the Vegas Golden Knights, why the GM of the Hurricanes is doing things no one has done before, whether the Canadiens have a chance, and the Nerd candy situation that Seth Jarvis has found himself in.
0:00 The gals welcome us in 3:02 NBA playoffs update 20:23 Fashion mailbag 24:18 DJ Bean talks Stanley Cup Final

entry.title: [UFS] Landon Donovan's Iconic Goal vs Algeria | The Greatest Moment in USMNT History? | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Reliving one of the most iconic moments in USMNT history.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard revisit the unforgettable match between the United States and Algeria at the 2010 World Cup — a game remembered for Donovan's dramatic stoppage-time winner that sent the USMNT into the knockout stage.
The two U.S. Soccer legends break down the play from start to finish, reflecting on the buildup, the outlet pass, the finish, and the emotions surrounding one of the most celebrated goals in American soccer history.
From the tension of the moment to the aftermath of the goal, Donovan and Howard provide a firsthand look at a defining chapter in USMNT lore.

entry.title: [SGS] How the New York Knicks are playing with the most immaculate vibes we’ve ever seen
entry.summary:
A little housekeeping: SGS merch is now available for purchase!
Madeline and Charlotte are back with another episode from New York City, which is alive with Knicks excitement. It’s hard to believe the Knicks are back in the Finals after 27 years, but it gets easier when you consider how immaculate the team’s vibes are. The gals talk about the Knicks’ stars, from Jalen Brunson to Karl-Anthony Towns, and the WAGs, from Jordyn Woods to Timothée Chalamet, and superfans, from Spike Lee to the ghost of JFK Jr. This is the sweet spot for Knicks fans!
Then, a few things: Giants’ QB Jaxson Dart introduced Donald Trump at a rally (yikes), Patriots offensive tackle Will Campbell is engaged to Ashlynn Nussmeier (the sister of Will’s former teammate Garrett Nussmeier), we got more photos from AJ Brown’s wedding, and Pep Guardiola’s estranged wife, Cristina Serra, went to watch his last match as Manchester City’s manager.
0:00 The gals have merch! 5:05 Knicks are back to the finals 18:17 Celebrity Knicks fans 29:33 Cavaliers implode 39:06 Jaxson Dart introduces Trump 42:51 Will Campbell engaged 46:38 AJ Brown wedding photos 48:17 Pep Guardiola’s estranged wife at his last game

Cox /Getty Images

entry.title: [UFS] Clint Dempsey: “Bury Me With The National Team Jersey” | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Clint Dempsey gets Unfiltered.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard are joined by fellow USMNT legend Clint Dempsey for a wide-ranging conversation covering his career, his upbringing in Texas, and some of his most memorable moments representing the United States.
Dempsey reflects on World Cup experiences alongside teammates such as Landon Donovan and DaMarcus Beasley, discusses former USMNT head coach Bob Bradley, and shares his perspective on what it means to wear the national team jersey.
The conversation also includes Dempsey's thoughts on which club Hall of Fame he would most want to be inducted into, the greatest player he ever played with, and the former teammates who would make his ultimate 5-a-side team.
#ClintDempsey #USMNT #UnfilteredSoccer #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #DaMarcusBeasley #BobBradley #USSoccer #AmericanSoccer #SoccerPodcast #WorldCup #SoccerLegends #FootballTalk #USMNTFans #RoadTo2026

entry.title: [PTFO] How to Win at Jeopardy! (and at Life) with Mina Kimes | PTFO
entry.summary:
After winning "Celebrity Jeopardy!" All-Stars 2026, Mina Kimes shares her strategy of overpreparedness with Pablo Torre and Domonique Foxworth: How is studying for a game show like playing in the NFL? What's the first rule of "Jeopardy!" Fight Club? And why would you bet zero dollars with a million on the line? Plus: Katie Nolan's secret pep talk, Daily Double brain glitch, Spygate... and a buzzer bibliography.

entry.title: [UFS] Yunus Musah's Costly Mistake + Moyes vs. Klinsmann | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
What happened to Yunus Musah's World Cup hopes?
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss Yunus Musah's position ahead of the FIFA World Cup and share their perspectives on the factors that have shaped his path with the USMNT.
The conversation also provides insight into how managers communicate lineup decisions, including the process of informing players about the starting XI before a match.
From roster battles to team management, Donovan and Howard break down another key storyline surrounding the United States as the World Cup approaches.

entry.title: [UFS] Chris Richards Injury Concern + Seamus Coleman's Everton Exit | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Chris Richards and Seamus Coleman headline this week's soccer discussion.
With Chris Richards dealing with an ankle injury ahead of the FIFA World Cup, questions remain about what the setback could mean for the USMNT defender and his preparations for the tournament. The injury also puts his availability for Crystal Palace's Europa Conference League Final into focus.
The conversation also turns to Everton, where longtime captain Seamus Coleman has announced that his 17-year run with the club will come to an end after the season.
From World Cup storylines to one of the Premier League's most respected careers, the episode covers two major developments in the soccer world.

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Major changes and major questions across the Premier League.
Liverpool are back in the headlines as Mohamed Salah publicly shared his frustrations with the current state of the club, adding another chapter to one of the biggest ongoing stories in English soccer.
Elsewhere, Manchester United have made a decision on their future by keeping Michael Carrick as the club's permanent head coach, while Chelsea have turned to Xabi Alonso to lead the next era at Stamford Bridge.
From managerial moves to one of the Premier League's biggest stars making headlines, this episode breaks down some of the most significant developments in European soccer.

entry.title: [UFS] USMNT's Revenge on Ghana | Reliving the Iconic 2014 World Cup Victory | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Reliving the USMNT's long-awaited World Cup redemption.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard revisit one of the most memorable victories in recent U.S. Soccer history — the United States' dramatic win over Ghana at the 2014 World Cup.
After World Cup eliminations at the hands of Ghana in both 2006 and 2010, the USMNT faced a familiar opponent once again in its opening match in Brazil. This time, however, the outcome was different.
Donovan and Howard look back on Clint Dempsey's early goal, John Brooks' dramatic game-winner, and the emotions surrounding a match that remains one of the defining moments of the 2014 tournament for the United States.
#USMNT #WorldCup2014 #Ghana #ClintDempsey #JohnBrooks #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #USSoccer #AmericanSoccer #WorldCup #SoccerHistory #USMNTFans #SoccerTalk #WorldCupMoments #RoadTo2026

entry.title: [PTFO] How Billionaires Ate Fans Alive, with Mike Schur & Joe Posnanski | PTFO
entry.summary:
Have billionaire owners left us in the twilight of American fandom? Are crying Liverpool fans actually the cure for male loneliness? And is the NFL offseason running out of juice? Or would you get married during the Super Bowl on Valentine's Day? Plus: Jim Thome's farm adjacency, Regis Philbin analytics, Gerry and the Pacemakers, institutional investors, the worst pandemic crowd... and feeding a sandwich to Stugotz on Microsoft Teams.
• Read "Big Fan": https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/778607/big-fan-by-michael-schur-and-joe-posnanski-foreword-by-tom-hanks/

entry.title: [SGS] Victor Wembanyama is the sports enigma that Aaron Rodgers wishes he was
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with an episode about a bunch of SGS main characters, but first: They start with the Western Conference Finals! The Thunder and the Spurs played a game that was more like Shakespeare than a basketball competition. San Antonio’s young star, Victor Wembanyama, is doing things on the court never seen before. He is also an enigma and has reached spiritual heights most of us only dream of.
Then: Tom Brady walked in a Gucci show that Alix Earle was at, José Mourinho is returning to Real Madrid as head coach and Pep Guardiola will leave his role as Manchester City manager this summer, Aaron Rodgers is returning to Steelers, NBA Rookie of the Year Cooper Flagg hard launched girlfriend and Duke University center, Arianna Roberson, Taylor and Travis were in Bushwick, and Eagles star AJ Brown got married to Kelsey Riley at Montage Laguna Beach. Were the flowers compressed? Listen to find out!

entry.title: [UFS] Jozy Altidore's USMNT Exit, Chris Richards Injury & Yunus Musah Questions | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Jozy Altidore gets Unfiltered.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard are joined by former USMNT teammate Jozy Altidore for a candid conversation covering some of the biggest moments and turning points of his career.
Altidore discusses the aftermath of the United States' failure to qualify for the 2018 FIFA World Cup, his experience playing for Sunderland, and the future of Yunus Musah within the U.S. Soccer program.
The episode also examines Chris Richards' injury situation ahead of the World Cup and Auston Trusty's role in helping Celtic add another trophy to its collection.
From USMNT storylines to club soccer headlines, Altidore joins Donovan and Howard for an in-depth look at the game.
#JozyAltidore #USMNT #UnfilteredSoccer #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #YunusMusah #ChrisRichards #AustonTrusty #USSoccer #CelticFC #AmericanSoccer #SoccerPodcast #FootballTalk #WorldCup2026 #RoadTo2026
In the AT&T Mailbag, the guys are diving into when players find out if they made the starting XI – and some very shady behavior to make sure they do.
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim drop every Tuesday. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. entry.title: [PTFO] Debunking Oz Pearlman's Tricks: Is He a Fraud? | PTFO
entry.summary:
He's wowed presidents, pro athletes and podcasters. But magicians tell us Oz Pearlman's viral act has crossed an ethical line. Stevie Baskin, after obsessively studying Pearlman's tricks, explains to Pablo Torre how influencers from Charles Barkley to the White House were in on the act — and why he thinks this brand of "mind-reading" amounts to fraud.
• Watch much more from Stevie Baskin: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FnwcU-XDyJ4
• Previously on PTFO: The Unwritten Rules of Magic https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J-efPrAa7i8
• Subscribe to Pablo's newsletter for exclusive access, documents and invites: https://www.pablo.show/
(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

entry.title: [UFS] Will Pulisic and McKennie Get the USMNT Ending They Deserve? | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Who could be next in line for a legendary USMNT send-off?
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss which current U.S. Men's National Team players are building the kind of careers that could one day earn a memorable farewell, with Christian Pulisic and Weston McKennie among the names featured in the conversation.
The two USMNT legends also debate which World Cup stars from other nations would make the biggest impact if added to the United States squad and what those players could bring to the team. Plus, Donovan and Howard recognize Lionel Messi's latest MLS milestone after the Inter Miami star became the fastest player in league history to reach 100 goal contributions.

entry.title: [UFS] Is Arsenal's Title Run Legit? | Unfiltered Soccer | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
VARSENAL? Arsenal, VAR, and Real Madrid drama take center stage.
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down Arsenal's latest Premier League result, including the controversial VAR decision that overturned a potential West Ham equalizer and helped preserve Arsenal's position at the top of the table.
The conversation also shifts to Spain, where the USMNT legends discuss the latest developments surrounding Real Madrid and one of the biggest storylines in world soccer.
From title races to controversy and club drama, Donovan and Howard weigh in on some of the game's hottest topics.

VARSENAL? Arsenal, VAR, and Real Madrid drama take center stage.

Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down Arsenal's latest Premier League result, including the controversial VAR decision that overturned a potential West Ham equalizer and helped preserve Arsenal's position at the top of the table.

The conversation also shifts to Spain, where the USMNT legends discuss the latest developments surrounding Real Madrid and one of the biggest storylines in world soccer.

From title races to controversy and club drama, Donovan and Howard weigh in on some of the game's hottest topics.

entry.title: [PTFO] Decoding the NFL vs. Rupert Murdoch (and TV Networks vs. Big Tech) | PTFO
entry.summary:
What do the tech titans of Silicon Valley and the old heads of traditional broadcast television have in common? Answer: they all need to kiss the ring of Roger Goodell. But will the NFL get too greedy in their continued pursuit of maximum return on their media rights? And why is Rupert Murdoch secretly meeting with Donald Trump? The Sporting Class — aka “Rich Guys OnlyFans” — returns…

entry.title: [SGS] Real Madrid is a mess: unhinged press conferences and teammates fighting while Kylian Mbappé laughs
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a real wild ride of an episode that starts with Tom Brady’s Mother’s Day posts. Then, NBA Playoff gossip: LeBron and the Lakers are out, so what will he do next? Stay in LA? Play for the Cleveland Cavaliers again? Retire? The Knicks’ vibes are insanely good and Philly got absolutely destroyed by a Josh Hart zinger. Plus: Daryl Morey is out as the 76ers president of basketball operations.
Luka Doncic put out an icky press release about his custody battle, Portland Fire upset NY Liberty at the buzzer, and F1 is taking employee feedback. And, finally: Real Madrid is an absolute disaster: Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni are fighting each other, Kylian Mbappé is laughing, and club president Florentino Perez held the most unhinged press conference the gals have ever seen. Which is saying something.
0:00 The gals say hello 2:41 Tom Brady update 6:11 NBA playoffs check-in 19:33 Fashion Mailbag 27:01 Daryl Morey is out 28:43 Luka Doncic’s press release 32:13 WNBA buzzer beater 36:01 F1 rules update 38:15 Real Madrid disaster

entry.title: [UFS] Should USMNT Fans Be Worried Before the World Cup? | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
The latest USMNT injury concerns ahead of the World Cup.
With the FIFA World Cup approaching, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down several key developments involving United States Men's National Team players competing at the club level. The discussion includes injuries to Christian Pulisic and Johnny Cardoso, what those situations could mean moving forward, and the latest surrounding their availability as the tournament draws closer.
The guys also discuss Gio Reyna finding the scoresheet for Borussia Mönchengladbach and what his recent form could mean for the USMNT.
From injuries and roster implications to performances abroad, Donovan and Howard cover the biggest storylines surrounding the United States ahead of the World Cup.

entry.title: [SGS] Do Caitlin Clark and Morgan Wallen actually need each other?
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with another deep dive, this time into Caitlin Clark and Morgan Wallen. The WNBA star recently walked out with the country singer at his concert in Indianapolis, and some fans are angry that she would associate herself with the controversial musician.
The gals go down a rabbit hole of Caitlin’s history of saying or not saying things when it comes to denouncing racism that some of her fans hurl at other WNBA players. They talk about Caitlin’s attempts at being apolitical and why those fail. Then they dive into Morgan Wallen’s history of being an athlete, the number of controversies he’s gotten himself into, and all the athletes he’s brought out at his concerts, including scandal-plagued guys like Mike Tyson, Brett Favre, Hulk Hogan, and more.
This episode even includes a deep dive into the tax records of Morgan’s charity and his attempts to sportswash his image through philanthropy. The gals then answer the question: What if Caitlin Clark and Morgan Wallen actually need each other?
0:00 The gals welcome us in! 2:39 Caitlin Clark’s history 17:55 Morgan Wallen’s history 41:10 Sportswashing and Morgan’s charity

entry.title: [UFS] Christian Pulisic Injured, Johnny Cardoso Surgery & Gio Reyna's Big Night | Unfiltered Soccer

author.name: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard

entry.summary:
Is Johnny Cardoso's World Cup in doubt?
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss Johnny Cardoso's recent ankle surgery and what it could mean for his chances of making the USMNT's World Cup roster. The conversation also covers Christian Pulisic's injury situation and Gio Reyna getting on the scoresheet for Borussia Mönchengladbach.
The guys also break down Arsenal's win over West Ham, including the controversial decision that disallowed a potential equalizer, before turning their attention to the latest developments surrounding Real Madrid and one of the biggest storylines in European soccer.
Plus, in the AT&T Mailbag, Donovan and Howard discuss former USMNT players who deserved bigger sendoffs, international stars they would have loved to see represent the United States, and what a co-ed all-time U.S. starting XI might look like.

entry.title: [PTFO] The Demise (and Future) of LIV Golf, Explained with Alan Shipnuck | PTFO
entry.summary:
Donald Trump may have ended his favorite sport's civil war — by waging war on Iran. But what did we really learn from Saudi Arabia's multibillion-dollar LIV Golf experiment? Journalist Alan Shipnuck explains what insiders are texting about but won't say out loud: that golfers are the most overpaid athletes on Earth, that peacetime is suddenly more complicated for the PGA Tour... and that sportswashing is insidious because it works.
• Read "LIV and Let Die" by Alan Shipnuck: https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/LIV-and-Let-Die/Alan-Shipnuck/9781668020012
• Previously on PTFO: Phil Mickelson, Trump and the "Big Daddy" Group-Chat Leak https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UbmBZR5aoRY
• Plus: Trump, Golf, and Saudi Arabia's Deal https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=e0o4KKO-fdE

entry.title: [SGS] WNBA season preview: which players are dating each other, WNBA-NBA couples, and off-season drama
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a WNBA season preview that only *they* could come up with: All the offseason drama due to the new collective bargaining agreement, and then a whole section about which players are dating each other and which players are part of WNBA-NBA couples.
The gals talk about the new expansion teams in Portland and Toronto, how the Aces and Liberty will be hard to beat, how A’ja Wilson will probably be a five-time MVP at the end of the season, why Angel Reese left Chicago, which GMs would take Paige Bueckers over Caitlin Clark, Lynx star Napheesa Collier and Lynx coach Cheryl Reeve’s coordinated PR campaign, and much more.
Then, relationships! A few include: Dewanna Bonner and Alyssa Thomas, Breanna Stewart and her wife Marta, Caitlin Clark and Connor McCaffery, and Natasha Cloud and Isabelle Harrison. Also — Dijoani Carrington and Nalyssa Smith broke up and now people think that Dijonai is dating Jackie Young. Plus: A’ja and Bam Adebayo went to the GQ Met Gala after party together in coordinating custom outfits, Angel and Magic’s Wendell Carter Jr., Kiki Rice and OKC’s Cason Wallace, and why it’s a great idea that Kelsey Plum and Napheesa Collier ended up with high-level athletic trainers.
0:00 Welcome to the 2026 WNBA preview! 4:26 New WNBA teams 10:13 Established WNBA teams 36:41 WNBA relationships
Subscribe to our YouTube channel: ‪@SportsGossipShow‬ Follow on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/sportsgossip Follow on TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/sportsgossipshow Listen on Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-sports-gossip-show/id1769969236 Listen on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/5tvzu1FzPdn8orKIv1OBQR Subscribe to our Substack: https://sportsgossipshow.substack.com/ Partner with us: https://www.sportsgossipshow.com/ Photo Credit: Getty Images Thumbnail Credit: Sam Hodde and Candice Ward/Getty Images
entry.content:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a WNBA season preview that only *they* could come up with: All the offseason drama due to the new collective bargaining agreement, and then a whole section about which players are dating each other and which players are part of WNBA-NBA couples.

The gals talk about the new expansion teams in Portland and Toronto, how the Aces and Liberty will be hard to beat, how A’ja Wilson will probably be a five-time MVP at the end of the season, why Angel Reese left Chicago, which GMs would take Paige Bueckers over Caitlin Clark, Lynx star Napheesa Collier and Lynx coach Cheryl Reeve’s coordinated PR campaign, and much more.

Then, relationships! A few include: Dewanna Bonner and Alyssa Thomas, Breanna Stewart and her wife Marta, Caitlin Clark and Connor McCaffery, and Natasha Cloud and Isabelle Harrison. Also — Dijoani Carrington and Nalyssa Smith broke up and now people think that Dijonai is dating Jackie Young. Plus: A’ja and Bam Adebayo went to the GQ Met Gala after party together in coordinating custom outfits, Angel and Magic’s Wendell Carter Jr., Kiki Rice and OKC’s Cason Wallace, and why it’s a great idea that Kelsey Plum and Napheesa Collier ended up with high-level athletic trainers.

0:00 Welcome to the 2026 WNBA preview!
4:26 New WNBA teams
10:13 Established WNBA teams
36:41 WNBA relationships

entry.title: [NFLNBC] 2026 Rookie Positional Rankings: Jeremiyah Love, Price, Tate | Fantasy Football Happy Hour (FULL)
entry.summary:
Matthew (@MatthewBerryTMR) details his time at the Kentucky Derby which included a strange interaction at dinner to Jay (@croucherJD) and Connor (@ConnorJRogers), then the trio dive into where Matthew is placing rookies in his positional rankings this season. Jeremiyah Love, Jordyn Tyson, and Fernando Mendoza are among the players discussed. The crew also highlights A.J. Brown, Jaxson Dart and other veteran movers in the rankings along the way and wrap up the show with their favorite bets to win Super Bowl LXI. #NBCSports #FantasyFootballHappyHour #NFL
Chapters: (0:00) - Welcome (1:45) – Matthew recaps his time betting Churchill Downs and shares an unusual story going out to dinner (7:30) – Rookie RB Rankings: Jeremiyah Love (RB11), Jadarian Price (RB27) (18:45) – Rookie WR Rankings: Carnell Tate (WR25), Jordyn Tyson (WR32), Makai Lemon (WR44), KC Concepcion (WR46), Omar Cooper Jr. (WR55) + Germie Bernard, Antonio Williams intrigue (31:40) – Notable Veteran WR Movers: A.J. Brown (WR11), DeVonta Smith (WR14), Terry McLaurin (WR22) (37:20) – Jay reveals why he is feverishly rooting for Arsenal to win the Premier League (40:50) – Rookie QB Rankings: Fernando Mendoza (QB28) + Jaxson Dart (QB9), Tyler Shough (QB17) among top veteran movers (44:40) – Rookie TE Rankings: Kenyon Sadiq (TE20) + Mark Andrews (TE12), Dallas Goedert (TE13) headline veteran shifts (50:05) – Last Call: Way too early Super Bowl LXI Champion bets

entry.title: [NFLNBC] Sophomore Jumps + Shedeur vs Deshaun | Chris Simms Unbuttoned (FULL Ep. 869) | NFL on NBC
entry.summary:
Chris is back from London…which celebrities did he meet in the swanky Arsenal suites? So will Travis Hunter play both ways again after all? Plus, Simms has some hard truths for the Shedeur Sanders fans, and he and Connor pick the 2nd-year players most likely to have a Sophomore Jump. #NFLonNBC #NFL #NFLDraft
Chapters: (0:00) “I feel like I’m detoxing at a rate I’ve never experienced before” (5:10) Simms is back from London, and has a slideshow! (18:40) Connor has lost the mustache (28:55) Jaguars GM says Travis Hunter “is set to play both sides of the ball” (42:30) Deshaun Watson or Shedeur Sanders as Browns QB1? (47:05) Emeka Egbuka on post-Mike Evans Buccaneers (52:40) DraftKings 2026 Receiving Over/Unders for 2nd-Year Players (53:30) Sophomore Jumps: Which 2nd-Year Players will Take the Leap this Year?

entry.title: [SGS] The MET Gala proves that sports have never been more important in pop culture
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a full discussion about this year’s MET Gala and why it clearly shows that sports have never been more important in the broader cultural conversation. Athlete attendees this year included Eileen Gu, Alysa Liu, Lindsey Vonn, Jimmy Butler, Joe Burrow, Justin Jefferson, Paige Bueckers, Angel Reese, A'ja Wilson, Ciara and Russell Wilson, Gabrielle Union and Dwayne Wade, Serena and Venus Williams, Naomi Osaka, and many more.
Then, a rundown of current NBA Playoff drama: The Celtics are out and both Joe Mazzulla and Jaylen Brown are coping, Steve Kerr and Draymond Green are fighting, the Knicks are looking good, LeBron is indefatiguable, and Victor Wembanyama continues to raise the bar for male emotional intelligence in sports.
0:00 The gals give us Devil Wears Prada 4:45 Met Gala and sports 27:05 Celtics cope 40:16 Steve Kerr-Draymond Green drama 46:05 Wemby gets in touch with his emotions
Subscribe to our YouTube channel: ‪@SportsGossipShow‬ Follow on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/sportsgossip Follow on TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/sportsgossipshow Listen on Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-sports-gossip-show/id1769969236 Listen on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/5tvzu1FzPdn8orKIv1OBQR Subscribe to our Substack: https://sportsgossipshow.substack.com/ Partner with us: https://www.sportsgossipshow.com/ Photo Credit: Getty Images Thumbnail Credit: John Nacion and Dimitrios Kambouris/Getty Images
