entry.title: [NFLNBC] Simms goes to London + 2026 Rookie Predictions | Chris Simms Unbuttoned (FULL Ep. 868) | NFL on NBC
entry.summary:
Welcome from London! Chris is across the pond enjoying his time learning Arsenal chants & drinking outside pubs, while Connor is at home…trying to grow something on his face? The guys do some Rookie Predictions and try to find this year’s Tyler Shough, Tetairoa McMillan, and more. Then, they go through some NFL news on Patrick Mahomes & Fernando Mendoza, before critiquing Malik Wills’ viral first pitch at a Marlins game. Plus, we take an impromptu poll of which Simms impression is better: Patrick Mahomes or Bane? #NFLonNBC #NFL #NFLDraft
Chapters: (0:00) “What do you have growing on your face?” (7:10) Chris learned an Arsenal chant (14:05) RIP Yankees legend John Sterling (16:25) 2026 Rookie Predictions: This year’s Cam Ward? Raiders QB Fernando Mendoza (20:35) This year’s Tyler Shough? Cardinals QB Carson Beck (24:20) This year’s TreVeyon Henderson? Cardinals RB Jeremiyah Love & Seahawks RB Jadarian Price (27:25) This year’s Quinshon Judkins? Commanders RB Kaytron Allen & Broncos RB Jonah Coleman (30:00) This year’s Cam Skattebo? Chargers WR Brenen Thompson & Buccaneers DB Keionte Scott (32:45) This year’s Tetairoa McMillan? Titans WR Carnell Tate, Saints WR Jordyn Tyson, Commanders WR Antonio Williams (37:05) This year’s Luther Burden III? Panthers WR Chris Brazzell II & Dolphins WR Chris Bell (39:40) This year’s Grey Zabel? Texans OL Keylan Rutledge & Ravens OL Vega Ioane (41:00) This year’s James Pearce Jr? Jets DE David Bailey, LB Malachi Lawrence, 49ers DL Romello Height (44:00) This year’s Abdul Carter? Chiefs DE R Mason Thomas, Ravens DE Zion Young, Patriots LB Gabe Jacas (47:15) This year’s Carson Schwesinger? Titans LB Anthony Hill Jr, Commanders LB Sonny Styles, Vikings LB Jake Golday (50:30) This year’s Nick Emmanwori? Cowboys S Caleb Downs & Browns S Emmanuel McNeil-Warren (54:10) Andy Reid says QB Patrick Mahomes is in “good position” to participate in OTAs (58:05) Fernando Mendoza learning to play from under center (1:02:30) Texans agree to 3-year extension with LB Azeez Al-Shaair (1:03:45) Why did the Lions decline Jack Campbell’s 5th-year option? (1:07:45) Surprise poll! Which Simms impression is better: Bane or Patrick Mahomes? (1:11:15) Malik Willis goes viral…why are QBs bad at throwing a baseball?

Steve Kornacki (@SteveKornacki) also stops by to highlight favorites and long shot wagers he’s making not only for the Kentucky Derby, but also Friday’s Kentucky Oaks. #NBCSports #FantasyFootballHappyHour #NFL

entry.title: [SGS] Jennifer Welch says sports can’t heal America
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are thrilled to be joined by Jennifer Welch, host of the “I’ve Had It” podcast with Angie “Pumps” Sullivan. Jennifer is an interior designer from Oklahoma City-turned probably the most influential progressive media personality today. She is the author of the book “Not Today, Fascists: The Lies That Got Us Here, and the Truths That Will Reunite Us” which will be out in the fall, and regularly interviews the most prominent politicians in the country.
The gals talk to Jennifer about how a Bravo show led her and Pumps to start their podcast, the grief that comes with deconstructing right-wing political beliefs, why Jennifer doesn’t think sports will bring us all together, her love for the Oklahoma City Thunder and Rafael Nadal, and much, much more!
0:00 The gals welcome us in 1:48 Interview with Jennifer 02:17 Jennifer Welch's background and move to NYC 06:18 Jennifer’s start in media and reality TV 08:58 Launching the 'I've Had It' Podcast 19:53 The role of sports in social change 36:44 Why sports won’t save America 45:19 Favorite tennis players and sports gossip

entry.title: [CD] Beijing Auto Show 2026: 8 GIANT SUVs. INSANE!
entry.summary:
At Beijing Auto Show 2026, over 1,400 cars were unveiled — but we're focusing on the 8 BIGGEST Chinese SUVs that are rewriting the rulebook. From a $22,000 budget giant to a $77,000 luxury flagship that makes Range Rover look like poverty spec, China is building some of the most impressive large SUVs on the planet.In this video, we walk you through the best massive SUVs from the Beijing Auto Show 2026 — covering design, tech, range, and price. Whether you're into off-road monsters, tech-loaded flagships, or incredible value, there's something here that will blow your mind.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ____________________________________________________________ ✅ Highlights:
Wuling Xingguang L — 6-seater under $22,000 with 260km EV range Nio ES9 — $77,000 flagship with 42-point massage & battery swap in 3 minutes Freelander — British name, Chinese tech, 5.1 meters of modern muscle Hongqi 1927 — 4 motors, 1,850 Nm, three locking diffs & 0-100 in under 4 seconds VW Unix 08 — Built on XPENG's G9 platform with VLA 2.0 ADAS & 700km range XPENG GX — 5.3 meters, 3000 TOPS compute, 750km range — the tech king Leapmotor D19 — Longer than BMW X7, starts at $32,000 — the value king BYD Grand Tang — 780hp, megawatt flash charging, 0-97% in 8 minutes

entry.title: [CD] NIO ONVO L80 is Perfectly BORING with MASSIVE storage!
entry.summary:
The ONVO L80 is the latest EV from NIO's sub brand. The ONVO L80 is a 5-seat variant of the previously popular ONVO L90. In this video, we take a quick yet thorough look at the L80 and explore how ONVO has reworked the L90 into a large 5 seat version.
This video covers all the major details of the ONVO L80, from its stylish green colour and frunk features to the massive trunk space that easily rivals the NIO ET5 Touring. We also take a look at the upgraded ADAS system, now with roof-mounted LIDAR, making the ONVO L80 an even smarter vehicle than the first ONVO L90.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ____________________________________________________________ ✅ Highlights:
Same dimensions as the L90 with a new green color Spacious 240-liter frunk & innovative camp kitchen Upgraded ADAS hardware, including roof-mounted LIDAR Impressive 17.3-inch rear entertainment screen 23-speaker sound system with 7.1.4 Dolby Atmos Efficient with 14.3kWh/100km for rear-wheel drive

entry.content:
The ONVO L80 is the latest EV from NIO's sub brand. The ONVO L80 is a 5-seat variant of the previously popular ONVO L90. In this video, we take a quick yet thorough look at the L80 and explore how ONVO has reworked the L90 into a large 5 seat version.

This video covers all the major details of the ONVO L80, from its stylish green colour and frunk features to the massive trunk space that easily rivals the NIO ET5 Touring. We also take a look at the upgraded ADAS system, now with roof-mounted LIDAR, making the ONVO L80 an even smarter vehicle than the first ONVO L90.

Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
____________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:

Same dimensions as the L90 with a new green color
Spacious 240-liter frunk & innovative camp kitchen
Upgraded ADAS hardware, including roof-mounted LIDAR
Impressive 17.3-inch rear entertainment screen
23-speaker sound system with 7.1.4 Dolby Atmos
Efficient with 14.3kWh/100km for rear-wheel drive

entry.title: [SGS] Mike Vrabel finally speaks out
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a new ep! First, relationship news: Guardians catcher Austin Hedges proposed on the field after win, Anna Hall married Giants wide receiver Darius Slayton, Gracie Hunt’s non-engagement party party, Sam Darnold’s wife Katie Hoofnagle Darnold gave the exclusive to Hailee Steinfeld’s newsletter Beau Society.
Tom Brady posted a shirtless Reel playing volleyball with his daughter. As one does! Mike Vrabel spoke to reporters about the Dianna Russini situation and the statement was as good as we’re going to get.
NBA Playoff drama —is this the end of the Warriors dynasty? Cleveland put out a questionable graphic. LeBron and his son Bronny did a promo for Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey, and Charlotte gives what is hopefully a correct history lesson. The Portland Trailblazers’ new owner, a guy named Tom Dundon, is super cheap?? The gals dive in.
And, finally, two other lessons in PR: Azzi Fudd and Paige Bueckers and the Dallas Wings communication people, and Lando Norris and the Guardian.

entry.content:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a new ep! First, relationship news: Guardians catcher Austin Hedges proposed on the field after win, Anna Hall married Giants wide receiver Darius Slayton, Gracie Hunt’s non-engagement party party, Sam Darnold’s wife Katie Hoofnagle Darnold gave the exclusive to Hailee Steinfeld’s newsletter Beau Society.

Tom Brady posted a shirtless Reel playing volleyball with his daughter. As one does! Mike Vrabel spoke to reporters about the Dianna Russini situation and the statement was as good as we’re going to get.

NBA Playoff drama —is this the end of the Warriors dynasty? Cleveland put out a questionable graphic. LeBron and his son Bronny did a promo for Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey, and Charlotte gives what is hopefully a correct history lesson. The Portland Trailblazers’ new owner, a guy named Tom Dundon, is super cheap?? The gals dive in.

And, finally, two other lessons in PR: Azzi Fudd and Paige Bueckers and the Dallas Wings communication people, and Lando Norris and the Guardian.

0:00 The gals say hello
3:21 Austin Hedges proposes
6:03 Anna Hall and Darius Slayton wedding
7:21 Gracie Hunt’s Sweetheart Ball
13:59 Sam Darnold’s wedding
15:51 Tom Brady’s volleyball reel
18:58 Mike Vrabel speaks
24:49 NBA playoffs
41:12 Azzi Fudd’s press conference
46:17 Lando Norris’ interview

entry.title: [SGS] Why the response to the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini story wouldn’t happen to a man
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with some updates: After photos leaked of NFL insider Dianna Russini holding hands with Patriots head coach, Mike Vrabel, Russini resigned from The Athletic. The gals talk about how male insiders in this industry cross ethical lines constantly and are not held to the same scrutiny and don’t suffer the same consequences. Plus: What’s going on with Mike Vrabel and the Patriots?
UNC football head coach Bill Belichick and his girlfriend, Jordon Hudson, attended a Savannah Bananas game. Speaking of UNC, former Denver Nuggets Michael Malone is the new head basketball coach, and his daughter is also a volleyball player at the school. Eagles offensive tackle Lane Johnson got married to Kelsey Kay Holmer — were the flowers compressed? Bears quarterback Caleb Williams was spotted at Coachella with Kendall and Kylie Jenner, which is sports.
WNBA free agency is in full swing and the gals have updates about Azzi Fudd, Satou Sabally, A’ja Wilson, and some damning stuff about the Chicago Sky. Amazon had “technical difficulties” during the very end of the very close, over time Hornets-Heat NBA play-in tournament game. Luka Doncic and Austin Reeves are out “indefinitely” for the Lakers with injuries, and Doc Rivers is out as the Bucks coach.
0:00 The gals sing a tune 2:14 Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini update 14:16 Bill Belichick-Jordon Hudson spotting 19:31 Michael Malone at UNC 21:46 Lane Johnson’s wedding flowers 24:59 Caleb Williams at Coachella 29:26 WNBA free agency 34:46 NBA updates

Henderson and Ethan Miller/Getty Images
entry.content:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with some updates: After photos leaked of NFL insider Dianna Russini holding hands with Patriots head coach, Mike Vrabel, Russini resigned from The Athletic. The gals talk about how male insiders in this industry cross ethical lines constantly and are not held to the same scrutiny and don’t suffer the same consequences. Plus: What’s going on with Mike Vrabel and the Patriots?

UNC football head coach Bill Belichick and his girlfriend, Jordon Hudson, attended a Savannah Bananas game. Speaking of UNC, former Denver Nuggets Michael Malone is the new head basketball coach, and his daughter is also a volleyball player at the school. Eagles offensive tackle Lane Johnson got married to Kelsey Kay Holmer — were the flowers compressed? Bears quarterback Caleb Williams was spotted at Coachella with Kendall and Kylie Jenner, which is sports.

WNBA free agency is in full swing and the gals have updates about Azzi Fudd, Satou Sabally, A’ja Wilson, and some damning stuff about the Chicago Sky. Amazon had “technical difficulties” during the very end of the very close, over time Hornets-Heat NBA play-in tournament game. Luka Doncic and Austin Reeves are out “indefinitely” for the Lakers with injuries, and Doc Rivers is out as the Bucks coach.

entry.title: [NFLNBC] Way Too Early WR and TE Rankings for 2026 | Fantasy Football Happy Hour (FULL)
entry.summary:
Matthew (@MatthewBerryTMR) updates Jay (@croucherJD), Connor (@ConnorJRogers), and Lawrence (@LordDontLose) on his recent vacation and the guys explain why Connor drinks free before dialing into Matthew’s way too early rankings for WRs and TEs in 2026. Justin Jefferson, Zay Flowers, and Ladd McConkey headlined the WR discussions while Colston Loveland, Tucker Kraft, and George Kittle are featured among TEs. To wrap up the show, the crew unveils their favorite bets for first and second WR to be drafted next week. #NBCSports #FantasyFootballHappyHour #NFL
Chapters: (0:00) - Welcome (1:00) – Matthew Berry recounts his European vacation, and the crew explains why Connor drinks free (6:20) – Jay highlights why he’s traveling overseas to see if a bet cashes (8:45) – WR Rankings #1-10: Justin Jefferson, Rashee Rice, Malik Nabers (17:10) – WR Rankings #11-20: Chris Olave, Garrett Wilson, Zay Flowers (26:15) – WR Rankings #21-30: Ladd McConkey, DeVonta Smith, DJ Moore, Luther Burden III, Rome Odunze (35:00) – WR Rankings #31-40: Parker Washington, Jakobi Meyers, Brian Thomas Jr., Michael Wilson (41:10) – TE Rankings #1-10: Colston Loveland, Tucker Kraft (45:55) – TE Rankings #11-20: George Kittle (48:15) – Last Call: Best bets for the 1st and 2nd WR Drafted

entry.title: [SGS] Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel: everyone is missing the point
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back after a short break last week to tackle the story everyone is talking about: The photos of NFL insider Dianna Russini and Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel. The gals talk about the public relations nature of the situation, the role that “insider” reporters play in sports, and the bigger picture of the media landscape that people need to consider when talking about the situation.
First, a few items about the WNBA Draft (more on that on Thursday), because former UConn teammates Azzi Fudd and Paige Bueckers were reunited on the Dallas Wings and…are they still dating? Rory McIlroy won the Masters for the second year in a row, and now he has to come up with a whole new menu for the special dinner. Plus: the Artemis II astronauts are the GOAT sports figures of our time.
0:00 The gals welcome us in 3:21 WNBA Draft recap 11:05 Masters debrief 16:28 Artemis II reactions 19:31 Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini

entry.title: [RTRS] The Sixers Are In The Postseason, What Do We Want Out Of It?

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary: The Sixers beat the Bucks, and the Magic lost to the backup Celtics, so the Sixers will host the play-in game on Wednesday in Philadelphia. We talk about what we’re hoping to get out of the play-in and playoffs, more on the Embiid and his appendicitis, an amazing clip of Michael Rubin talking about the White Party, and Mike gets to hear the new Doc Rivers song as he gets fired. entry.title: [RTRS] Joel Embiid's Appendix Surgery, Sixers Lose To The Rockets, Doc Rivers Is Full Of It (Again)

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
Joel Embiid has had many different injuries for the Sixers, but this is his first appendicitis. Joel Embiid had surgery on Thursday, just hours before the game against the Rockets. The Sixers will likely be without him for the play in and first round of the playoffs, if they make it. We talk about the situation, and the loss to the Rockets.

entry.title: [CD] NIO ES9: Germany Has NO ANSWER To This Chinese SUV!
entry.summary:
The NIO ES9 is the SUV version of NIO's flagship ET9 sedan, combining high-tech luxury with the space and versatility of a large SUV. With its commanding road presence, 6 seat configuration, and a tech-forward interior, it might just be the answer to the growing demand for luxury EV SUVs. But how does it perform? Does it live up to the luxury SUV competition, or is it just another overhyped tech showcase?
In this video, we explore the NIO ES9’s design, features. Do you really need to spend top dollar for a luxury Rolls, Bentley or Aston to get the best of the best? I don't think so.

- Stunning road presence - 600L trunk space - Brand new 47-speaker sound system with Dolby Atmos - Instant intelligent privacy glass - 42-point Spa Massage - 20-point foot massage! ______________________________________________________________

entry.title: [SGS] We found a shocking new development in the Athlete Wedding Industrial Complex
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with another episode — the only one for this week because they are taking a tiny break! First, a few March Madness updates: UCLA won the women’s tournament and the Michigan men beat UConn with five transfers. Geno Auriemma went after Dawn Staley and the apology he issued was…not great!
The Masters starts on Thursday and golfer Jason Day is making waves with his Malbon outfit, which involves birds and fish (complimentary). Plus: Rory McIlroy, Scottie Scheffler’s nine-day-old son, and more. Tiger Woods is going to rehab in Switzerland? Bills quarterback Josh Allen and his wife, actress Hailee Steinfeld, had their baby and went to Seahawks Quarterback Sam Darnold’s wedding. Gracie Hunt (daughter of Kansas City Chiefs owner Clark Hunt) had a compressed flower engagement that exposed a new problem in the athlete wedding industrial complex: votives. Track star Noah Lyles got married to Junelle Bromfield, a Jamaican track and field athlete, at the same place NBA star Kemba Walker got married.
Angel Reese is being traded from the Chicago Sky to the Atlanta Dream, which is great for Atlanta and stupid for Chicago. Italy didn’t make their third-straight World Cup, Cleveland Browns head coach Todd Monken missed the NFL coach’s photo because he was getting his haircut for the photo, and Lakers star Luka Doncic is going to Spain to deal with an injury.
0:00 The gals are in different states! 6:26 March Madness finishes 22:36 The Masters preview 36:56 Josh and Hailee welcome their baby 39:42 Gracie Hunt’s engagement 44:42 Noah Lyles and Junelle Bromfield’s wedding 46:32 Angel Reese to Atlanta 49:20 Italy misses World Cup 50:14 NFL coaches photo 52:30 Luka’s treatment

entry.content:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with another episode — the only one for this week because they are taking a tiny break! First, a few March Madness updates: UCLA won the women’s tournament and the Michigan men beat UConn with five transfers. Geno Auriemma went after Dawn Staley and the apology he issued was…not great!

The Masters starts on Thursday and golfer Jason Day is making waves with his Malbon outfit, which involves birds and fish (complimentary). Plus: Rory McIlroy, Scottie Scheffler’s nine-day-old son, and more. Tiger Woods is going to rehab in Switzerland? Bills quarterback Josh Allen and his wife, actress Hailee Steinfeld, had their baby and went to Seahawks Quarterback Sam Darnold’s wedding. Gracie Hunt (daughter of Kansas City Chiefs owner Clark Hunt) had a compressed flower engagement that exposed a new problem in the athlete wedding industrial complex: votives. Track star Noah Lyles got married to Junelle Bromfield, a Jamaican track and field athlete, at the same place NBA star Kemba Walker got married.

Angel Reese is being traded from the Chicago Sky to the Atlanta Dream, which is great for Atlanta and stupid for Chicago. Italy didn’t make their third-straight World Cup, Cleveland Browns head coach Todd Monken missed the NFL coach’s photo because he was getting his haircut for the photo, and Lakers star Luka Doncic is going to Spain to deal with an injury.

0:00 The gals are in different states!
6:26 March Madness finishes
22:36 The Masters preview
36:56 Josh and Hailee welcome their baby
39:42 Gracie Hunt’s engagement
44:42 Noah Lyles and Junelle Bromfield’s wedding
46:32 Angel Reese to Atlanta
49:20 Italy misses World Cup
50:14 NFL coaches photo
52:30 Luka’s treatment

entry.title: [RTRS] Reaching The Zugzwang, Connie And Jack, Barely Beating Nets

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
On today's show, Spike and Mike discuss MOC’s article on if the Sixers have reached the Zugzwang, referenced in the Hinkie letter, that describes the situation where you’re forced to make a move that will worsen your position. MOC proposes that the Sixers have three main options moving forward. Mike is joined later on by Bo Belanger, creator of Connie and Jack: A Rigged Game, a podcast that follows Connie Hawkins, basketball superstar who is blackballed from the NBA for a crime he didn’t commit. Oh, and the Sixers barely beat the Nets.
MOC article: https://www.rightstorickysanchez.com/p/have-we-reached-the-zugzwang Connie and Jack: https://www.connieandjackpod.com/

entry.title: [RTRS] Live Ricky From New Orleans featuring Jake Fischer, Amos Lee, and Mia Kang

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
The game at Fly The Process was a disaster, but the trip was amazing. We did the pod live from the legendary Preservation Hall in New Orleans, with special guests Jake Fischer, Amos Lee, and our official poet Mia Kang. We talked about the team's struggles, Morey, Nurse, the team's future and the guy who got his identity stolen on Bourbon Street when he was drunk.

entry.title: [CD] Zeekr 7X Family Road Trip: NOTHING Went As Planned!
entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me via Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
Join me as I take the Zeekr 7X on an epic 800 km road trip through the heart of China! This Swedish-designed, China-driven electric SUV is ready for the long haul, but can it survive three days and 12 hours in the car with my family? Spoiler alert: It’s more than up to the task.
With the Zeekr 7X, expect a mix of stunning views, unexpected adventures, and some seriously smooth road trip vibes. From winding mountain roads to the vibrant streets of Chenzhou, I’ll show you what this EV can do while keeping my family entertained and comfortable.
But is the Zeekr 7X truly the perfect road trip companion or just another overhyped electric SUV? Let’s dive into the experience and find out!
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
✅ Highlights:
• Sleek Swedish design, packed with tech and luxury • fast-charging capabilities • Dual-motor all-wheel drive, smooth power delivery • Family-friendly with spacious seating and entertainment • Impressive NVH (noise, vibration, harshness) control for quiet driving • A mix of scenic drives, local eats, and cultural experiences • Real-world charging experience in remote areas

Join me as I take the Zeekr 7X on an epic 800 km road trip through the heart of China! This Swedish-designed, China-driven electric SUV is ready for the long haul, but can it survive three days and 12 hours in the car with my family? Spoiler alert: It’s more than up to the task.

With the Zeekr 7X, expect a mix of stunning views, unexpected adventures, and some seriously smooth road trip vibes. From winding mountain roads to the vibrant streets of Chenzhou, I’ll show you what this EV can do while keeping my family entertained and comfortable.

But is the Zeekr 7X truly the perfect road trip companion or just another overhyped electric SUV? Let’s dive into the experience and find out!

Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇

✅ Highlights:

• Sleek Swedish design, packed with tech and luxury
• fast-charging capabilities
• Dual-motor all-wheel drive, smooth power delivery
• Family-friendly with spacious seating and entertainment
• Impressive NVH (noise, vibration, harshness) control for quiet driving
• A mix of scenic drives, local eats, and cultural experiences
• Real-world charging experience in remote areas

entry.title: [CD] Zeekr 9X UNLEASHES 1400HP Beast That DESTROYS Luxury SUVs
entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me via Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
I finally got behind the wheel of the all-new Zeekr 9X — the biggest, boldest, most luxurious Zeekr yet.
With its massive chrome grille, brutalist styling, and tri-motor, 1,030 kW Hyper Edition, some have called it the “Chinese Cullinan.” But the 9X isn’t a copy — it’s Zeekr’s own flagship statement, packed with tech, performance, and a luxury interior that rivals anything Europe has on offer.
But is the Zeekr 9X really worth its 480k–570k RMB price tag? Is it a true luxury SUV benchmark or just a spec-sheet flex? Let’s find out.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
✅ Highlights:
• 900V SEA Super Hybrid Platform with 6C charging (20–80% in 9 mins) • Up to 1,030 kW power, 0–100 km/h in 3.1 seconds • Tri-motor Hyper Edition + dual-motor Max/Ultra trims • Dual-chamber air suspension & 48V active roll stabilization • 32-speaker, 3,868 W Naim Audio 9.2.4.8 Dolby Atmos system • Sliding 17-inch OLED rear entertainment screen • Swiveling zero-gravity second-row seats with 22-point massage • 3-row cabin with real wood, metal accents, and nappa leather • Active road & engine noise cancellation, NVH near EV levels • Zeekr G-Pilot ADAS, Level 3–ready on Hyper spec

entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me vis Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
I finally got behind the wheel of the brand-new NIO ES8. The third generation of NIO’s flagship SUV.
The original ES8 was NIO’s statement piece, but it came at a steep price. Now, NIO has dropped the cost massively, packed in more tech, more features, and more luxury, and is gunning straight for the world’s premium SUV market. Range Rover, GLS, X7… consider yourselves on notice.
But is the new ES8 just cheaper, or has NIO actually built a flagship that can reset what we expect from a luxury SUV? Let’s find out.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ___________________________________________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
Massive price drop vs. the outgoing ES8
Battery-as-a-Service (BaaS) option slashing costs further
900V architecture with 600 kW peak charging
Flagship luxury interior, 6-seat and 7-seat layouts
Smart ADAS with NOA on Chinese highways
Huge upgrade in performance & handling
Tech-heavy cabin with new screen setup ___________________________________________________________________________________________

entry.title: [CD] China’s EV Makers Make a SHOCKING U-Turn – Here’s Why
entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me vis Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Email us at ChinaDriven123@gmail.com We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
Big boy SUVs are everywhere at the Chengdu Auto Show 2025. Over five meters long, three rows, flagship status — it seems every Chinese brand is rolling out a massive NEV SUV this year. But why so many new hybrids, and not pure EVs?
This show may not be as flashy as Beijing or Shanghai, but Chengdu gives us the perfect chance to step back and look at the trend. From Geely’s Galaxy M9 to Yangwang, Zeekr, and others, Chinese OEMs are clearly betting that hybrids are the safe play in the market.
So what does this mean for the EV transition in China? Are things slowing down for pure EVs?

entry.title: [CD] XPeng P7: The EV That CHANGED China RETURNS!
entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me vis Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
I finally got behind the wheel of the stunning XPeng P7, the original Xpeng P7 car kickstarted my obsession with Chinese EVs. The original P7 Performance was a sleek, rebellious sports sedan in a sea of sensible crossovers… but after a softer Xpeng P7+ follow-up, many wondered: was the magic gone?
Well, this new P7 Performance is sharper, faster, smarter and possibly even better looking. But does it still have that original spark? Or is it just another facelift with a fancy spoiler?
Let’s find out.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
___________________________________________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
Dual-motor AWD with 595 kW & 695 Nm
0–100 km/h in 3.7 seconds (claimed)
Active aero + adaptive suspension
New interior with swiveling 15.6” screen & 87" AR HUD
Up to 820 km range (CLTC) with fast 5C charging
Three Turing AI chips + Snapdragon 8295 for cockpit & ADAS
___________________________________________________________________________________________

entry.title: [CD] ONVO L90: ONLY $20,000...Maybe NIO's BEST SUV
entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me vis Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
🚗 I’m back in the ONVO L90 — this time to drive it.
After a full walkaround in my previous video, we’re now taking this premium, three-row electric SUV for a spin in the mountains of Chongli, Hebei. With a 900V architecture, swappable batteries, a bigger frunk than the Lucid Gravity, and a price that undercuts the Model Y... it might just be the best-value EV on the planet.
But how does it actually drive? And can a sub-$30K (with BaaS) family SUV really deliver on luxury, performance and practicality?
My 7-year-old has thoughts. So do I.
🎥 If you haven’t already, check out my static review for the full breakdown of the ONVO L90’s design, tech, storage, and interior: [insert link]
Highlights and Chapters below 👇 ___________________________________________________________________________________________
✅ Driving Highlights:
Smooth and quiet ride quality — even in mountain conditions
Premium cabin materials and class-leading third-row space
Frunk is bigger than a Lucid Gravity’s… but missing one thing
Voice-activated features, massage seats, dual 3K screens — even in the back
Did ONVO nail the family EV formula?
___________________________________________________________________________________________

I’ve finally got my hands on the ONVO L90, a bold new full-size electric SUV that promises to deliver a perfect balance between performance, practicality, and luxury. With 590 horsepower and some seriously impressive features, ONVO has clearly been listening to what consumers want.
In this car reivew, I put the L90 to the test, exploring its design, performance, and practicality in the real world. How does it stand up against the competition? And does it truly deliver the ideal family SUV experience?

entry.title: [RTRS] Ace Bailey Bails On Sixers, Big Boards Of Yesteryear, The Two Timeline Problem with Sam Esfandiari

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
Ace Bailey decided to cancel his workout with the Sixers, we discuss the implications if any. Then CJ Finds Out as he goes back in time to go over some of our Big Board hits and misses of the last decade, Sam Esfandiari of the Light Years (Warriors) podcast joins us to talk about the pitfalls and trouble with the 'two timelines' plan that the Sixers are sort of doing, and we discuss whether TJ McConnell could be Finals MVP.

entry.title: [CD] This Geely EM-I Hybrid gets INSANE Fuel Economy!
entry.summary:
2.6L/100km | 100mpg - Real-world test in CRAZY Chinese MEGA-City Chongqing!
I finally got behind the wheel of the Geely A7 EM-i, one of Geely's hybrid cars powered by the brand-new E-motive Intelligent Super Hybrid system — their answer to BYD’s DM-i.
Geely claims this next-gen system delivers super low fuel consumption, smarter energy management, and stronger performance. But is it just another hybrid… or the smartest PHEV on the market right now?
In this video, I take the EM-i system on a proper real-world test at 30% SoC through Chongqing’s traffic, city roads, and highways to see what it’s really capable of. How efficient is it when the battery runs low?
Spoiler: it surprised me.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ___________________________________________________________________________________________ ✅ Highlights:
Geely’s latest EM-i Super Hybrid system explained
Try to beat the claimed 2.67L/100km fuel economy
Real-world efficiency tested on city + highway routes
Smooth EV mode driving, seamless engine transitions
Geely's Answer to BYD DM-i dominance ___________________________________________________________________________________________ ⏱️ Video Chapters:

entry.title: [CD] NIO ET5 Touring: Why it's a GAME-CHANGER!
entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me vis Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
I drove the updated 2025 NT2.5 NIO ET5 — one of China’s best-selling premium electric sedans, now with sharper looks, tech upgrades, and some much-needed tweaks to the drive. It’s still a sleek, sporty liftback, but is it finally the driver’s EV it always promised to be?
With improved range, faster charging, and subtle design changes, is this the sweet spot in NIO’s lineup? And should Tesla Model 3 and Zeekr 7GT buyers be worried?
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ___________________________________________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
0-100kph in 4.0 seconds
Upgraded horizontal screen & Nomi 3.0
Refined chassis and NVH improvements
New headlights & aero tweaks
Revised interior materials and trim + new seats
First drive thoughts — sharper, more refined, still a looker

entry.title: [CD] NIO ET9 - Forget German Luxury, This Is INSANE!
entry.summary:
Interested in importing a Chinese EV or Hybrid? Contact me vis Whatsapp +86 18570089464 We can connect you with our experienced Car Export partner.
I FINALLY got my hands on the NIO ET9 — NIO’s most advanced electric flagship to date, loaded with wild tech, 900V architecture, and rear seats that would make business class blush! It’s huge, and touts the world's most advance suspension system, but does it drive like a proper luxury GT, or is it just a tech demo on wheels?
With NIO planning to take the ET9 global, should this be the EV to finally tempt you out of your SUV? Would you buy one over a Maybach or a BMW i7?
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ___________________________________________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
0-100 in 5.3 seconds (for a 3.1 ton, 5.3m long luxury barge!)
900V fast-charging architecture
Full steer-by-wire + rear-wheel steering
Active suspension with hydraulic roll control
Multi-screen ultra-premium interior
First drive thoughts — surprising agility for its size

entry.title: [CD] NIO Firefly: The Mini EV Europe Should Fear
entry.summary:
Here’s my first look at NIO's Firefly, a compact electric hatchback from China’s latest EV brand. With a funky light design, electric powertrain, and premium feel not found on many small Chinese cars, can the Firefly compete in the global EV market? Will Firefly be able compete in the land of hatchbacks in Europe?
I take it for a spin, exploring its performance, tech, and how it feels on the road.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ___________________________________________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
* Compact, sleek design with modern touches
* Impressive driving performance for its class
* Clever hidden storage compartments
* Minimalist, Premium interior
* First drive thoughts – Small car, Big drive
___________________________________________________________________________________________
⏱️ Video Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
00:45 - Price and Market Position 01:58 - Firefly’s Competitors
02:57 - Battery, Range & Swapping
03:26 - Firefly Exterior Styling
05:00 - Global EV Development
05:25 - Firefly Frunk & Trunk Space
6:22 - Firefly Interior (Front) 12:29 - SURPRISE! 12:47 - Firefly Interior (Rear)
14:54 - Power, Acceleration, Top Speed 15:10 - Driving Impressions
18:13 - Firefly ADAS 19:11 - Cool Driving features
20:05 - Outro
___________________________________________________________________________________________
Would you choose the Firefly over other hatchbacks in the same segment?
📍 Filmed in Suzhou, China 🎥 Subscribe for more real-world EV reviews, straight from the streets of China.
